title: The One After "I Do"

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Great. Great! Just give me a sec to change film.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I know I’m not supposed to know, but I do. And I’m so excited for you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? What’s going on?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica’s pregnant!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! Is that why you guys had to get married?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys! I’m not pregnant.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah. Slow swimmers?
lines.stage_direction: To Chandler Chandler looks at him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! What do you mean? You-you-you’re not pregnant?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You didn’t tell anybody I was did you?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! I’ll be right back.

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Now why don’t we get a shot of just Monica and the bloody soldier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, about that. Joey, you have to change before the party.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can’t! I-I don’t have any other clothes here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Find some! Please! Anything that doesn’t say I-I died tragically in France.

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Well then why don’t we see the bride and the groom and the bridemaids.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Hey Mon, why did you tell the guys you weren’t pregnant?
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe and Rachel join Monica and Chandler on the altar.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because I’m not.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We found your test in the trash, if you’re not pregnant——It’s because I am.
lines.stage_direction: She sees Rachel shaking her head

scenes.scene_description: The Wedding Hall, Monica and Chandler have just said "I do," and the photographer is taking the required pictures. First of Monica, Chandler, Ross and Joey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, I…I am with child. And I didn’t want to say anything because it’s your day; I didn’t want to steal your thunder.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute! So you told people I was pregnant?! Does this look like a conversation that I want to remember?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who’s the father?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t say.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t say because he’s famous.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, who is it?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, come on, you have to tell us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, okay. It’s James Brolin. James Brolin is the father of my baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: As in Barbara Streisand’s husband James Brolin?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! Well he never said that to me!

scenes.scene_description: Flash, the photographer takes a picture of Monica and Chandler’s stunned faces.

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Why don’t we have Monica step away and we’ll get Chandler and the bridemaids.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How about just the bridemaids?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know I am the groom right? I was told it was kinda big deal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: For you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thank you for doing that. I just can’t deal with this just quite yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So instead you told me Monica was pregnant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You said that she was, I just didn’t disagree with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sneaky.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Smile ladies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh by the way?
lines.stage_direction: They smile and the picture is taken.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-hmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: James Brolin?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I know. I could only think of two names, him and Ed Begley Jr. and then I remembered he’s gay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ed Begley Jr. is not gay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?!

scenes.scene_description: The Wedding Hall, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Bandleader
lines.text: Thank you very much! Ladies and gentlemen, it gives me great pleasure to introduce to you for the very first time, Mr. and Mrs. Chandler Bing!

scenes.scene_description: The Reception Hall, the party is in full swing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Before we go out there I’ve got a present for ya.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, I’m going to put my hand in your pocket!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’ve been taking dancing lessons.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, the last six weeks. I wanted this to be a moment you will never forget.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that is so sweet!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So? Would you care to join me in our first dance as husband and wife?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes.

scenes.scene_description: They enter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What’s the matter?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t know, it’s these new shoes, they’re all slippery.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, are you going to be able to do this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not well.

scenes.scene_description: They walk onto the dance floor and Chandler slips and almost falls.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, the good news is, I don’t think anyone’s looking at us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, are you ready to talk about it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, we’ll talk about something else then.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who’s the father?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh! Look honey y’know what? I haven’t told him yet, so until I do I don’t think I should tell anybody else.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. That’s fine. That’s fair. Is it Tag?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I’m sorry. I’ll stop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is it Ross? It’s Ross isn’t it—Oh my God, it’s Joey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, stop it! I am not going to tell you until I tell him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah-hah! At least we know it’s a him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh sweet Lord.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m sorry! Okay? I went down to the gift shop and it’s either this or a bathrobe! Look, what’s more important, the way I’m dressed or me being with you on your special day?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, I’m not even going to pretend I was listening. Hey! Hey!
lines.stage_direction: Sees someone else. Goes over to that person.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. I’m uh, I’m Ross. I don’t, I don’t believe we’ve met. I’m Monica’s older brother.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh hi, I’m, I’m Mona from her restaurant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Hello uh, Mona from her restaurant. Mona, wow what a, what a beautiful name.
lines.stage_direction: He uses his card to mouth those words. Pause

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: You think so? I’ve always kinda hated it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aw come on, Mona Lisa?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mona umm…Clickclocken. The famous botanist? Huh? Oh no she’s uh—well she’s dead now. No, supposedly she was once quite the hottie of the plant world.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Really?! Well see? I never knew about her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Linda Clickclocken. So what uh, what-what table are you at? Oh, uh me too.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh good. Now there’ll be someone there who likes my name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes there will. Oh guess what, Molly Gilbert you’ve just been bumped up to table one. And if it’s all right with you I’m gonna take your place at table six—Martin Clickclocken.
lines.stage_direction: sexily Mona leaves and Ross tries to find the same table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s better, now just bend your arms a little more. There you go. Okay, look straight ahead. Now this time I want you to really put your ass into it.
lines.stage_direction: They do a practice swing and she really puts her ass into it.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Chandler darling! Look, my date has finally arrived. I’d like you to meet Dennis Phillips.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Congratulations.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Dennis is a dear old friend and a fantastic lover.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bravo Dennis thanks for pleasing my mother so.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Oh, I’m so sorry I missed the ceremony, I was stuck at auditions.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Oh yes, Dennis is directing a new Broadway show.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t believe we’ve met, Joey Tribbiani.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Dennis Phillips

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow, I’ve admired your work for years. You-you’ve done some really amazing stuff.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Oh, thank you. Well if you’ll excuse me, I’m gonna go get myself a drink. Be back in a moment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow Dennis Phillips! That’s great! How did you guys meet?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Well, it’s a funny story.

lines.speaker: Funny
lines.text: ha-ha or funny:

lines.speaker: Bandleader
lines.text: Thank you, thank you very much. If everyone will please take your seats, dinner will be served.

scenes.scene_description: They start dancing and Chandler starts slipping around.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Uh, I thought, I thought you were at table six.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: No, nine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh see, before you uh, when you showed it to me you-you held it that way which uh, which was misleading. Well I’m… Hello.
lines.stage_direction: he turns her hand upside down He goes at sits down at his new table and the kids stare at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler. Will you see if your mom can give my resume to Dennis Phillips? ‘Cause if I can get in a Broadway show then I would’ve done it all, film, television, and theater. The only think left would be radio, and that’s just for ugly people.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What size shoes do you wear?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, eleven, eleven and a half.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great, because my shoes are giving me a little problem on the dance floor, can I borrow the boots from your costume?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, I don’t even really know where I left those. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Those aren’t eleven and a half.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay fine! I’m a seven! All right, I have surprisingly small feet. But the rest of me is good, I’ll show ya!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can you believe Phoebe got pregnant?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh y’know what honey? Let’s not talk about that right now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so huge.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure, but come on, as big as your wedding?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course not nothing is. Between me and you…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: …in this day and age how dumb do you have to be to get pregnant?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Y’know, sometimes you can do everything right, everyone can wear everything they’re supposed to wear, and one of those little guys just gets through!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know! Maybe they have tools.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well I-I talked to and uh, she’s definitely going to have this baby. Y’know, she said she was gonna raise it on her own.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, maybe that’s, maybe that’s really brave.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe. I just hope she realizes how hard it’s gonna be.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Maybe she hasn’t really thought it through that well.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, there’s a lot to think about. I mean, how is she, how is she going to handle this financially? How is she going to juggle work? Does she realize she’s not going to have a date again for the next eighteen years?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-hmm. I’m just thinking about Phoebe; poor knocked up Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Champagne?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yes! Thank you very much! Oh that’s-that’s actually how the French drink it.
lines.stage_direction: She grabs a glass, takes a sip, and realizes what she just did. She then tries to spit the champagne back into the glass without Monica noticing. It doesn’t work.

scenes.scene_description: Ross starts looking for table six and finds out that it’s the kid’s table. He sees Mona sitting at another table.
Monica gasps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I just got off the phone with my lover, James Brolin…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, and apparently he is married to some singer, but he said he would leave her for me. And I said, "James, James Brolin, are you sure?" James Brolin said…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel’s really the one who’s pregnant.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! Why bother?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How do you feel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know. I don’t know how I feel. This is all happening so fast. I have to make all these decisions that I don’t want to make. Somebody just take this away from me!!
lines.stage_direction: Takes another sip of champagne and spits it back out

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Calm down. Maybe you’re not pregnant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: When I got pregnant with the triplets, I took that test like three times just to make sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Maybe it’s a false positive. Are you sure you peed on the stick right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How many ways are there to do that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m-I’m just saying, don’t freak out until you’re a hundred percent sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I’ll-I’ll take it again when I get home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You-you gotta take it now. Come on, do it as a present to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’ll run out and get you one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you guys are so great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, wait a minute! Who’s is the father?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, she won’t tell us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, come on it’s my wedding! That can be my present.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wh—Hey, I just gave you peeing on a stick.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See? This is why you register.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was the chair again! Okay? I’m not doing it! It what—look, I don’t—y’know what—eh-eh… Hi.
lines.stage_direction: He walks away and goes over to Mona.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, would you like to dance?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh great!
lines.stage_direction: They get up to dance and Ross is interrupted by a little girl.

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Dr. Geller?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I wasn’t farting! Uh, a little game from our table. Yes?
lines.stage_direction: To Mona To the little girl

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Dr. Geller, will you dance with me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh umm, well uh, maybe-maybe later. Right now, I’m about to dance with this lady.

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: She drops her head in disappointment and walks away.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Ohhhh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, unless! Unless, uh this lady wouldn’t mind letting you go first.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I’d be happy to. You are very sweet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes I-I am. In fact umm hey, why don’t we try it my special way? You can dance on my feet.

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Hop on. Is the pretty lady looking?
lines.stage_direction: They start dancing and Mona sits down.

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Keep dancing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And the world will never know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Did you talk to Dennis about me yet?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, I told him how talented you were. I told him all about Days Of Our Lives .

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no! No! No! You don’t tell a Broadway guy that! Now he just thinks I’m a soap actor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But you’re not just a soap actor. You are a soap actor with freakishly tiny feet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no, thank you Miranda.

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Melinda!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: How cute was that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, were you, were you watching?

scenes.scene_description: continued from earlier, only now Phoebe joins them.

lines.speaker: Second Girl
lines.text: Can I go next?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Of course you can! Hop on!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay, but I get to hop on after her.
lines.stage_direction: Ross bites the air in response.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am so gonna score.

lines.speaker: Second Girl
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I like your bow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’d like to propose a toast. To Monica and Chandler, the greatest couple in the world. And my best friends. Now, my when I first found out they were getting married I was, I was a little angry. I was like, "Why God? Why? How can you take them away from me?!" But then I thought back over all our memories together, some happy memories. And-and there was some sad memories. I’m sorry. And-and some scared memories—Whoa! Eh? And then, and then I realized I’ll always be their friend, their friend who can speak in many dialects and has training in stage combat and is willing to do partial nudity. Oh! To the happy couple!
lines.stage_direction: clinks his glass overly angry Does a fake laugh. Starts to break down and cry. He jumps back, startled. Starts to walk away, but realizes something.

lines.speaker: Bandleader
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That was very nice Ashley.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: So, is it my turn now?

scenes.scene_description: Another little girl walks over to him.

lines.speaker: Fat Girl
lines.text: I’m next!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Uh, that’s okay. You can dance with her first.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you-you sure? Okay. Okay. So what’s uh, what’s your name.
lines.stage_direction: She nods yes. To the girl

lines.speaker: Fat Girl
lines.text: Gert!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s, that’s pretty. Whoa! Whoa! Whoa! What are you doing there Gert?
lines.stage_direction: They start to dance and Gert tries to step on Ross’s feet, but he pulls them out of harm’s way.

lines.speaker: Gert
lines.text: Dancing on your feet! Like the other girls did it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Hop on Gert.
lines.stage_direction: Swallows hard. She does and Ross winces in pain.

lines.speaker: Gert
lines.text: Why aren’t you moving your feet?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m trying.
lines.stage_direction: He strains to move his feet.

lines.speaker: Gert
lines.text: Faster! You’re not going fast enough!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe I should stand on your feet!
lines.stage_direction: Gert’s shocked and Ross realizes what he said and tries to brush it off.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So did you uh, happen to catch my toast up there?

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Oh my God, that was for my benefit?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I’d like to think there was something for everyone. Look, I know you’re casting for this new show…

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Look umm Joey, I-I don’t think you’re quite right for this project.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, see that’s where you’re wrong. Whatever it is I can do it. And if didn’t see it up there, just-just try me.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: It’s an all Chinese cast. Can you be Chinese?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I’m not proud of this, but…
lines.stage_direction: He turns around and starts to mess with his eyelids.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Oh my God! No-no-no! Please! Please! Don’t-don’t-don’t!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, are you ready to get back on the dance floor?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did it turn into sand?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh come on, I love this song! Come on, you’ll be fine.
lines.stage_direction: She starts to walk towards the floor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. No, I won’t. Do you know why I took all those lessons? See, for the first time I didn’t want you to be embarrassed to be seen on the dance floor with some clumsy idiot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh sweetie, you can never embarrass me. Okay, you can easily embarrass me. But come on, it doesn’t matter. All right? I married you! So I want to dance on my wedding night with my husband. Come on. Just try not to move your feet at all. There you go.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler grunts. They go onto the floor. Chandler starts to get into the groove and bust a move.

scenes.scene_description: A large little fat girl walks over.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Chandler, I’m gonna have you arrested.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: You stole my moves.
lines.stage_direction: He starts to dance like Chandler was and Chandler stops.

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Geller dances over.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How much longer?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 30 seconds.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: 30 seconds, okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did I miss it? Rachel, I-I want you to know that, if it’s positive, we’re gonna…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh I know. I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s time.

scenes.scene_description: The Women’s Restroom, Rachel and Phoebe are waiting for the outcome of Rachel’s second test.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Go ahead Rach.

scenes.scene_description: Another woman starts to enter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wait! Y’know what? I can’t, I can’t look at it. I can’t. Somebody else tell me, somebody tell me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, it’s negative.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s negative.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh. Well there you go. Whew! That is—that’s great—that is really great-great news. Y’know ‘cause the whole not being ready and kinda the financial aspects, all that. Whew. Wow, this is so just the way it was supposed to be. God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well… Well, great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thanks. God this is so stupid! How could I be upset over something I never had? It’s negative?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it’s positive.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s-it’s not negative, it’s positive.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well yeah, I lied before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now you know how you really feel about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh, that’s a risky little game!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you really gonna do this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. I’m gonna have a baby. I’m gonna have a baby. I’m gonna have a baby!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: With who?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, it’s still not the time.

scenes.scene_description: She goes over to look.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just didn’t see the fast song coming.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Shh. Shh. Don’t try to talk, we’ll get you up to your room, we’ll soak your feet, you’ll be okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thank you.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: That is so sweet!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: No, I mean it. There are so few genuinely nice guys out there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Tell me about it, I feel like I’m holding down the fort all by myself.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: It’s Joey right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute! No! I’m the nice one! I’m the one who danced with the kids all night! How…How small are your feet?!

scenes.scene_description: The Reception, Joey is helping Ross walk after Gert got through with him and Mona is looking on concerned.

title: The One With The Red Sweater

lines.speaker: Directed by
lines.text: David Schwimmer

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Listen y’know what sir? For the last time, I don’t care what the computer says, we did not take a bag of Mashuga nuts from the mini-bar and we did not watch Dr. Do-Me-A-Little!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Joey uh, were you in our room last night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. I was told the name of the movie would not appear on the bill!
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe grabs the receipt and shows it to Joey who gets mad.

scenes.scene_description: The Hotel Lobby, Rachel and Phoebe are at the front desk checking out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi! We’re checking out of the bridal suite.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s right. I’m no longer a bride. I’ll never be a bride again. Now, I’m just someone’s wife!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And I’m the happiest guy in the world! Oh honey, come on don’t be upset. We still have so much to look forward to!
lines.stage_direction: Monica goes and sits down in a huff.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah, right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We got the honeymoon.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s not ‘til Thursday.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The wedding pictures?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They won’t be ready for weeks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not the disposable cameras from the tables.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s true! I knew I married you for a reason!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’ll tell you what, I will go get them developed and you can go home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica enter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What? What did you take a picture of?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing! It was something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay Ross has the cameras, has he checked out yet?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you joking? Check out is not ‘til noon and he has a good eleven minutes left.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross has never checked out of a room a minute before he had to.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, one time, when we were dating, uh we got a late checkout, he got so excited it was the best sex we ever had. Until y’know, he screamed out Radisson at the end.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, well I’m gonna get Ross, get the cameras, and get them developed. 32 Joe. You’re 32!

lines.speaker: Front Desk Clerk
lines.text: Here’s a copy of your bill.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh thanks. Champagne, strawberries…Oh my God! I can’t believe Chandler ordered porn on our wedding night!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, that’s sad. Mashuga nut?

scenes.scene_description: Joey giggles.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey. Soaps? Shampoos? Are you really taking all this stuff?
lines.stage_direction: He sees that Ross is packing all of the hotel toiletries

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why not? It’s built into the price of the room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah but you don’t need——What is this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thread!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Score! Where are the disposable cameras?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What disposable cameras?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The cameras? Remember last night I told you to take them?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No you didn’t.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! Remember? Right before we cut the cake, I went up to you and I said…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh yeah, you-you came up to me and asked if I could do you a favor, and my Uncle Murray came up to you and handed you a check. And then you said, "Why do they call it a check? Why not a Yugoslavian?" Yeah, then you did that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you don’t have the cameras?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. Sorry man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So? What? What? They’re gone! Monica’s gonna freak!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I’m sure they’re still somewhere here in the hotel. I’ll-I’ll help you look for them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: In-in three minutes.

scenes.scene_description: Ross’s Hotel Room, he is letting Chandler in.
Chandler goes into the bathroom, closes the door, and then opens it again right away. Ross looks up and hands him the toilet paper Ross already packed.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you gonna open the presents without Chandler?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! But, they’re callin’ out to me! I mean this little guy even crawled up into my lap. Oh come on, Chandler wouldn’t mind if I opened just one present! What do you think it is?
lines.stage_direction: Pause Holds up a small one

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A little mirror that when you look into it you see yourself as an old woman.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Phoebe is entering to see Monica sitting in front of a mound of wedding gifts.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: A tiny salt shaker!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh! My God! For tiny salt!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh wow! Okay. Well that was fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Good. Okay, I’m just gonna wait for Chandler to open the rest of them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Whew. Although y’know, this is part of a salt and pepper set. I mean… I guess y’know it may just count as a half a present. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well I guess it’s okay to open one more if it’s part of a set. Y’know, it’s probably this one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or this one!
lines.stage_direction: She grabs and starts to open the biggest present.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, how are you feelin’? Any morning sickness?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shh-shh-shh! The guys don’t know yet do they?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Joey and Ross don’t know anything and Chandler still thinks that Phoebe’s pregnant.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah that’s right Chandler does still think I’m pregnant. He hasn’t asked me how I’m feeling or offered to carry my bags. Boy, I feel bad for the woman who ends up with him. After you of course.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don’t worry I promise that you will only have to be pregnant for a few more hours, ‘cause I’m going to tell the father today.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, is it someone in this building? Is it that tall guy from the first floor?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ew! No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! I think he’s cute.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then you have his baby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Believe me I’m trying.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow. Y’know it is so weird. I mean, you’re gonna tell this guy today and he has no idea what’s gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. You’re just gonna knock on his door and change his life forever. You’re like Ed McMahon except without the big check, or the raw sexual magnetism.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Uh-huh, I guess it is pretty big news.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Pretty big? It’s huge! God, this guy doesn’t have a clue! He’s just walking down the street thinking, ‘I had sex with Rachel Green. I rock!’ then bam! He’s a father and everything’s different.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well it’s only different if he wants it to be. I mean, I’m not gonna ask him for anything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Then he still has this huge decision to make. Now he’s walking around thinking, ‘Do I want to be a dad?’ and then bam!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What was that bam?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t. He got…he-he-he-he’s hit by a bus.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey, what would you do if someone that you slept with told you that she was pregnant?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who called here? Did she sound blond? Huh? Did-did-did she have an accent? I gotta make a call! I shoulda never walked into that Sunglass Hut!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Joey! Joey! No, it’s not you! You didn’t get anybody pregnant!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Why would you scare me like that? What the hell is going on? Is somebody pregnant?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah. That’s me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God Pheebs! You’re gonna have a baby?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes. Yes I am. Oh my God, I’m gonna have a baby!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, wait a minute. Who’s the father?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don’t know him. It’s not important. He wants nothing to do with me or the baby.
lines.stage_direction: She sits down like she’s pregnant.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well who is this guy?! Huh? Who is he? ‘Cause I will track him down and kick his ass!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: David Lynn.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: David Lynn! David Lynn! David Lynn!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who’s David Lynn?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh some guy from my gym. A little annoying.

scenes.scene_description: Monica opens it anyways.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Did you find the cameras?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. Did you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! And that’s why I’m under the table. Celebrating.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I checked in the uh, lost and found, I talked to the manager, no-one’s turned them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well this is great. Y’know, those cameras were the only thing that was gonna cheer Monica up today, she’s really depressed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now you guys just got married, why is she so depressed?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All my energy is going into not asking that question. I can’t believe I screwed this up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m sorry man. Here’s a thought. This is the same ballroom. There’s a band. There’s gonna be plenty of dressed up people.
lines.stage_direction: Formally dressed people start to enter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you suggesting we dance our troubles away?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no-no, I’m saying we-we buy more of this at the gift shop, throw our tuxes back on, and take a few pictures. All we have to do is make sure not to get anybody else’s faces.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m just thinking about your new bride at home. Okay? Do-do you really want to start your life together by letting her down?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Marriage advice? Really?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m telling you, this looks exactly like your wedding! Aren’t these the same flowers?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t know, Monica picked out the flowers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What about the chairs?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She picked those out too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How about the place settings?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What did you do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was in charge of the cameras! Gift shop?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm.
lines.stage_direction: They head off to the gift shop.

scenes.scene_description: The Banquet Room, Chandler is under one of the tables as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Ooh, did you do it yet?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well what are you doing here? Are you about to do it? Is it Gunther?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Phoebe, it’s not Gunther.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank God, ‘cause that hair on a baby…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe the father is not here okay? I haven’t told him yet and I don’t think I can tell him at all now!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know, let me think. I was walking down the street thinking, ‘I’m gonna tell the father today’ and then bam!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bus?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, you! Phoebe you freaked me out. You kept saying how huge this all is!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well-well but it is huge.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, but I was just thinking about how huge this is for me. I didn’t even go to how huge this was going to be for the father.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re thinking about this way too much. Just tell him and get it over with. It’s like, it’s like ripping off this Band-Aid. Quick and painless, watch. Oh mother of…See?
lines.stage_direction: On her arm Rips it off.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Rachel is sitting on the couch as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh-ooh-ooh! Are we opening presents?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No! I shouldn’t have even opened these! I mean I—Joey I am out of control!! Joey, you have to do me a favor. No matter what I say, no matter what I do, please do not let me open another present! Okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Give me one more.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh good, uh you’re here. Uh Pheebs? Listen uh sit down. I-I got something I want to say.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right.
lines.stage_direction: She sits down like she’s pregnant again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm, now uh… It’s a scary world out there, especially for a single mom. Y’know, now I always thought you and I had a special bond so… Phoebe Buffay, will you marry me?
lines.stage_direction: He goes to one knee and pulls out a ring.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Joey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hell yeah! I’ll marry you!
lines.stage_direction: She grabs the ring and puts it on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You can’t marry him!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey lady, your day’s over! It’s my turn!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why?! Why can’t she marry me?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can and I will!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She’s not pregnant. It’s Rachel. Rachel’s the one who’s pregnant.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe I think he would notice if you didn’t have a baby in nine months!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s Joey!
lines.stage_direction: Joey turns and looks at her and she mouths ‘I love you’ to him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now I can’t believe it! What? Rachel’s pregnant? Who’s the father?
lines.stage_direction: smiles then stops The girls nod yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We don’t know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh… I wonder if that dude.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There’s a dude?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who? Who is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: About a month ago this guy spent the night with Rachel, I didn’t see who it was but…
lines.stage_direction: He walks out and closes the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Was that story over?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Obsessive Monica has now opened more of the wedding gifts.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The guy left this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! I know who the father is…
lines.stage_direction: gasps She walks into Monica and Chandler’s.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: People have got to finish their stories!

scenes.scene_description: They follow him and meet him in the hall coming out of his apartment carrying a sweater.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, excuse me? Could you take a picture of us?

lines.speaker: Woman At The Wedding
lines.text: Oh! Of course.
lines.stage_direction: Ross and Chandler pose and she takes the picture.

lines.speaker: Man At The Wedding
lines.text: Uh, would you take one of us?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhh… Yeah sure. Click!
lines.stage_direction: Holds the camera up to his face.

lines.speaker: Woman At The Wedding
lines.text: It didn’t click.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I heard it. I heard it.

lines.speaker: Man At The Wedding
lines.text: But there was no flash.

lines.speaker: Woman At The Wedding
lines.text: Why won’t you take our picture?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah. I’ll take, I’ll take your picture.
lines.stage_direction: He takes the picture with his finger over the lens.

lines.speaker: Man At The Wedding
lines.text: Uh, your finger was covering the lens.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who are you? Ansel Adams?! Get outta here!

scenes.scene_description: The Banquet Room, Ross and Chandler are in their tuxes and have started to fake the pictures.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look, I feel really bad about how I freaked you out before, so I called the father and asked him to meet you here so you can tell him. Go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Hey wait a minute! Phoebe, how do you even know who the father is?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I may play the fool at times, but I’m a little more than a pretty blond girl with an ass that won’t quit. I believe this belongs to the father of your baby.
lines.stage_direction: She takes the sweater out of her purse.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God… Oh, he’s in there right now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh. Now you can turn around or you can go in there and rip the Band-Aid off. What to you want to do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, let’s rip!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really? Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, sorry. Yeah.
lines.stage_direction: They go inside to confront the father.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Hey Rach.

scenes.scene_description: Outside Central Perk, Rachel and Phoebe are about to go inside.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why don’t you go up on stage. I’ll get a picture of you doing the speech.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay!

scenes.scene_description: The Banquet Room, Ross is taking a picture of a plant, Chandler a fork. The band stops.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Will the owner of a 1995 Buick LeSabre please see the front desk? Your car is about to be towed.

lines.speaker: Anxious Wedding Guest
lines.text: That’s my car!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A ’95 LeSabre?!

lines.speaker: Anxious Wedding Guest
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A green LeSabre?

lines.speaker: Anxious Wedding Guest
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m sorry, I meant a blue LeSabre.

lines.speaker: Anxious Wedding Guest
lines.text: Yes! Green-blue!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well go! Go move it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, you ready for the last picture?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Get ready to run. Congratulations on your wedding.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler walks over to the new bride. He grabs her, kisses her, Ross takes the picture, and they both run out.

scenes.scene_description: He goes up on stage, mimes like he's giving the speech, and Chandler takes his picture. However, before he gets down everyone starts clinking their glasses for a real speech.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: So, what’s this about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel has something that she wants to tell you and umm, I believe that this is your red sweater.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: No. This is my red sweater.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no. Could I get anyone a coffee or…poison? No? Just for me? Okay.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What’s going on Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing! Phoebe kinda made a mistake. But y’know you do wear that sweater a lot, are you involved in some kind of dare?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Y’know, I’m actually glad Phoebe called. I know we broke up because you thought I wasn’t mature enough, but I’ve really grown up and think we should get back together.
lines.stage_direction: He pulls out a stool and Rachel sits down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it’s just not the right time.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: It is the right time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I’m ready for more.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Tag…

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Come on Rach, let’s give it another try.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m having a baby.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You can go.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Thank you.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t know how any of these got opened?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You opened them all?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! I know! I am a terrible person! I mean, Chandler is never going to trust me with anything ever again!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey! You got my parent’s gift!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. What is that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I don’t know. I think it does something to salami.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Obsessive Monica has finished opening all the presents. She’s ashamed of this, at least, because as someone enters…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! How’d it go?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. What-what did Tag say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Tag is not the father! And Joey knows now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I do Rach. I do, and I so happy for you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow, you didn’t even try to unhook my bra!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So are you ever gonna tell whoever it is?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I will. I’m just not up for it tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach listen, no matter what this guy says I want you to know you’re not gonna be alone in this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen I uh… It’s a scary world out there especially if you’re a single mom. Y’know, I always felt like you and I have this-this special bond. Y’know? So, Rachel Green will you marry me?
lines.stage_direction: He takes her hand. gets down on one knee again

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.text: What?!!

lines.text: No!!

lines.text: No! Joey, oh you’re so sweet. You’re so-so sweet, honey. But I’m not, I’m not looking for a husband.

lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now, if you will excuse me I am going to go and lie down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t say that didn’t hurt. But I’ll take you back Joey Tribbiani.

lines.text: Uh yeah. Pheebs, listen about that. I only offered…

lines.text: Ooh! A Salami Buddy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There you go!

lines.text: Here’s a picture of Ross. And that’s me. And that’s me and Ross. Oh-ho, that is a picture of our first kiss as a married couple.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! That is a great picture!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Oh and interesting because I found the cameras in one of our bags!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh, didn’t see that coming.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so this isn’t a picture of our first, but it is a picture of my first kiss with…with this lady. Which by the look on your face I’m sure you’ll remember. So we don’t need——There’s no need to have this picture. How about I take the real pictures and get them developed right now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That would be a good idea.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. You opened all the presents without me?! I thought we were supposed to do that together!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You kissed another woman!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Call it even?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.text: Well, I’m gonna go get these in some water.
lines.stage_direction: the floral bouquet he walked in with

lines.text: Wait you stole those from these people’s wedding?

lines.text: No-no, I took them from the hotel lobby. Yeah, they think they can charge me for some dirty movie and a bag of Mashuga nuts, they got another think coming. Hey! My sweater! I’ve been looking for this for like a month!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! How you doing?

lines.text: Good! Good, long time no see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Like your sweater.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hey, right back at ya.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Oh, it’s crazy about Rachel huh?

lines.text: Yeah. She—Well, she’s one crazy lady?

lines.text: Umm, I don’t some Italian guy. Come on, read your own label. See you later.

lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He is so weird.

title: The One Where Rachel Tells...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Babe! Aren’t you excited we’re going on our honeymoon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah I am!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bermuda, Bahama, come on pretty mama …

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s right. Get it out of your system while we’re alone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Have a great honeymoon!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’d better go pack.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, I already packed. The only thing I couldn’t find though was your Speedo.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A Speedo? Uh, I don’t have a Speedo. I’m gonna go pack my regular long bathing suit.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh good you’re still here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I want to tell you to have a good honeymoon!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I also wanted you guys to know that I am telling the father today. What? What? What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We know its Ross!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How?! How do you know?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It was his sweater, but—Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I so wanted Ross to know first, but I’m so relieved you guys know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so great! And I’m gonna be your baby’s aunt!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me too!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m gonna be an uncle! Come here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You’re all gonna be aunts and uncles.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but I’m the only one related by blood.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Great! So now that you guys all know you can help me. Give me some advice on how I’m gonna tell Ross!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what were you gonna say?

lines.text: Well I was gonna tell him that I’m-I’m gonna have the baby and he can be as involved as he wants.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well that, that sounds good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah but how do I start? I mean, what’s-what’s the first thing that I say? Okay great! Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! Good luck!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, what was that all about?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well I guess there is no harm in telling you now, Rachel and Ross are gonna have a baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! I didn’t even know that—Why didn’t you tell me?! Why am I talking like this?!
lines.stage_direction: in a high pitched voice Pause

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I didn’t think you could keep it a secret.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! I am an excellent secret keeper. I have kept all of our secrets.
lines.stage_direction: in the high pitched voice Normal voice

lines.text: What secrets?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no Joey, I am not going to tell you because I am an excellent secret keeper.

lines.text: You’ll tell me later?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You already know.

lines.text: Oh, it's so romantic to send people off on their honeymoon.

lines.text: Y’know, Monica and Chandler are married. Ross and Rachel are having a baby. Maybe you and I should do something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All in good time my love. All in good time. Oh shoot! I left my guitar in their apartment. Well you can let me in later.

lines.text: I don’t have a key, they took mine to give to you.

lines.text: What?! They took mine to give to you!

lines.text: Hey, I washed those!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No you didn’t.

lines.text: Yeah that didn’t sound like me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, what am I gonna do? I really need my guitar!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I have stuff in there too.

lines.text: What stuff?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica’s chicken parm! I’ll take care of it. Hey Mr. Treeger, it’s Joey Tribbiani. Listen, I need to get into Monica and Chandler’s apartment. It’s an emergency. Uhh, gas leak! Yeah oh, and bring garlic bread.

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Did Rachel find you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh she was looking for you.

lines.text: Yeah, it seemed pretty important.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think I might know what this is about.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Uh, uh we promised we weren’t gonna tell anybody this but uh, about a month ago Rachel and I slept together.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! I thought you would be a little more shocked.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh sorry. And?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, we-we said we’d just do it that one time but, but now I think she may wanna start things up again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I don’t think that’s what it is.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why? What-what else could it be?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh wow, I don’t feel well.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m telling you. I’m telling you. That’s what it is. No wonder she was looking at me all funny during the wedding. She didn’t say anything to you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe it’s something you ate?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Please, just-just, just go and talk to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I guess I should. Man, y’know what I have to realize? Maybe I’m just not the type of guy women can have just one night with. Y’know, they-they always seem to want a little bit more. I should remember that.

lines.text: Next?

lines.text: 25J and K, any chance those aren’t together?

lines.text: What the hell is that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa! Whoa! Whoa! Treeger, what are you doing?

lines.text: You said there was a gas leak in here.

lines.text: Well why don’t you use your key?

lines.text: Because by the time I find it on this thing , the whole place might have exploded. If that happens at another building that I manage, people are gonna start asking questions. Come on! Hurry up.

lines.text: Oh! We could have done that.

lines.text: No.

lines.text: Fine. Go! Go! Go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi. Umm, I think there’s something that we really need to talk about.

lines.text: I think we do. Why don’t we go inside? Look uh, I know why you’re here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, and to save you from any embarrassment umm, I think maybe I should talk first.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Uh, Ross and Rachel. Rachel and Ross. That’s been one heck of a see-saw hasn’t it?

lines.text: What?

lines.text: I mean look, that-that one night we had was fun and…and certainly passionate, but don’t you think it’s better if we just stayed friends?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Seriously. What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay. Y’know what? If you want to, we can do it one more time. I mean I’d-I’d be okay with that. In fact, I have some time right now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y’know what? Can I, can I talk now?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m pregnant. Ross? Ross? Okay, whenever you’re ready. And you’re the father by the way—but you got that…
lines.stage_direction: touches his knee Ross stops. Ross is staring off into space. Ross is still frozen Sits back and opens her magazine.

lines.text: Can I get you some water?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m good. I’m good.

lines.text: Yeah, I need uh… I’m just—I don’t know—I don’t understand, umm, how this happened? We-we used a condom.

lines.text: I know. I know, but y’know condoms only work like 97% of the time.

lines.text: What? What? What?!! Well they should put that on the box!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They do!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No they don’t!!! Well they should put it in huge black letters!!!!

lines.text: Okay Ross come on let’s just forget about the condoms.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh well I may as well have!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Listen, y’know what? I was really freaked out too when I found out…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Freaked out? Hey no, I’m not freaked out! I’m indignant ! As a consumer !

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know what? Let’s, let’s talk later.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! I want to talk now! Okay? I—In fact, I am going to talk to the president of the condom company!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y’know maybe I should come back…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Shh! Shh! Shh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah I’ll press 1!

lines.text: What?!

lines.text: You’re gonna get pregnant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I screwed up so bad, I told Monica that I would stuff and send all these wedding invitations like weeks ago and I-I…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You didn’t do it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I-I know—I had put them in…in-in my desk at work and I completely forgot about them until today.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, the scene is continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sweetie okay. It’s okay. Everybody made it to the wedding. I’m fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Kinda hurtin’ my hand though.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I cannot believe that I did this. Especially after Monica just went on and on and on about it! "Okay Rachel! Here are the invitations Rachel! Now be very careful Rachel! Please, drinking no liquids around the invitations Rachel!" Whoa oh! Oh-oh-oh! Oh…oh-oh-oh…
lines.stage_direction: on tape Ross hands her a glass of wine Mimicking Monica She tilts her wine glass above and moves it back and forth across the invitations

scenes.scene_description: Chandler is shocked and Rachel gets scared of Monica very quickly.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did you do it on our invitations?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not on the ones we sent out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, just the ones gave back to us and we had framed!
lines.stage_direction: Slams the framed invitation down onto the table.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel hits fast forward. Monica is completely shocked.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can you believe this is already happening? I mean it seems like yesterday they just got engaged.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. Hey remember…remember the night they got engaged? How uh, you and I almost…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I remember how we almost. Do you think we would’ve gone through with it? Y’know, if we hadn’t gotten caught. Do you think we would’ve done it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean I…I know I wanted to. I just, I just wasn’t sure if you wanted to.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh I wanted to.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel stops fast forwarding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So we…we both wanted to.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Interesting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Anyway umm, it probably worked out for the best.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, in about ten seconds you’re gonna see him kiss me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And in about five seconds you’re gonna see why.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross did I ever tell you about the time that I went backpacking through Western Europe?

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Rachel trade looks while watching the tape.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, get ready to see some beggin’!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you came on to Ross!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now I’m so happy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you talking about?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You used the Europe story!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s the magic story you use when you wanna have sex!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How do you know about that story?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How do you know about that story?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I heard it from my friend Irene who heard it from some guy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Some guy!!
lines.stage_direction: raises his hand Points to himself.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. No, she told me his name was Ken Adams.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ken Adams!!
lines.stage_direction: raises his hand and points to himself again

scenes.scene_description: Joey’s shocked and everyone else looks at Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So uh, apparently people are familiar with the Europe story?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Listen about that, the whole uh, who came on to who thing really doesn’t matter. I mean, I think it would’ve happened either way. I mean if you hadn’t initiated it I-I-I know I would’ve.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It was an amazing night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was. It was an amazing night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You think it looked amazing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I uh… I don’t know. I mean I…I honestly didn’t watch it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, me neither. Yet…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhhhhhh…that-that may be weird.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it would be really weird.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is looking at the tape sitting on the counter as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good luck to you.

scenes.scene_description: Ross grabs the tape and heads for the VCR as Rachel goes over and puts the chain on the door and locks it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mind if I mute?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, oh there go the clothes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are undressing very quickly.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Six months Rachel, six months.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey. We-we look…we look pretty good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s what I was gonna say.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh nice tan!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! I had just gone to the beach that weekend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah….

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Have you been working out?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I have been working out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really? Wow, this is so much better than I…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh that’s not pretty.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, make it stop!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Make it stop!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Have to make it stop!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!!!

scenes.scene_description: Ross pushes play.
They both get out of the chair and run for the VCR.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello Greg? Hi, this is Monica from the plane? Listen, the number that you gave me 853-5… That is their old number! Jenny’s been giving it out since they moved!
lines.stage_direction: on phone Listens Listens To Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Jenny! That is so Jenny!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey listen umm, how would you like to get together? Say next Saturday? Okay, how about Sunday? Okay umm, the week after that? The week after that? Y’know what Greg? Y’know what? We are good, interesting, funny people with good questions and if you and your precious Jenny can’t see that then… January 15 th ? We’ll see you then! Okay!
lines.stage_direction: on phone Listens Listens Listens Listens Listens Chandler dances. Listens

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler have gone through the phone book and found Greg and Jenny’s number which Monica has just dialed.
She hangs up the phone and they hug.

title: The One With Rachel's Date

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, now this one is rare, this one is medium well! Now go-go-go! Hey Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey how was dinner?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Dinner was good!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m just saying hi! Now I’m gonna go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

scenes.scene_description: Monica’s Restaurant Kitchen, she is frantically working and is handing two finished dishes to a waitress.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well hello there.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I didn’t see this on the menu.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh Tim? This is Phoebe. Phoebe this is Tim, my new sous chef.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, so you're Monica’s boss?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Actually she’s my-my boss. Sous is French for under.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! I sous stand.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Tim? I need a calamari and a Caesar salad. And umm, could you get me the pesto?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh you…you made pesto?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Yes I did.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Would you say your pesto is the best-o?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: I…I-I don’t know, but I would say it’s pretty good-o.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe turns to leave but notices an attractive man.
Phoebe laughs too hard.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I still need a calamari and a Caesar salad.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: I like your necklace.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I made it myself.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: You are so talented.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it’s no pesto.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, all right! Let’s just cut to the chase, okay? You’re single. You’re single. He gets off work at eleven. She’ll be waiting for your call. I’ll give him your number if I can get one calamari and one Caesar salad!! I did not yell. I am not putting a dollar in the jar.
lines.stage_direction: To Phoebe To Tim To Phoebe To Tim To Phoebe Everyone in the kitchen stops.

scenes.scene_description: Monica goes over and grabs the pesto.

lines.speaker: Mr. Franklin
lines.text: Wow Bing! Burning the midnight oil.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know me sir. Oh ah, I do have a question for ya. Do you know how I get around the office computer network so I can access the really good Internet porn?

lines.speaker: Mr. Franklin
lines.text: You’re a joker Bing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What’s funny about that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Sorry I kept you waiting so long.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey that’s okay. So, where do you want to go?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh ah, I think you know where I want to go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The Hard Rock Caf� ?

lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Again?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!

lines.text: I’m telling you, I like the food!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You like the Purple Rain display! Hey Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hey Toby! Have a good night.

lines.text: Did that guy just call you Toby?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, he thinks that’s my name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, why don’t you correct him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh it’s been going on way to long now. Y’know, I mean the first time he said it we were just passing each other in the hallway, so I didn’t say anything. And then the next time he said, "Hey Toby, do you want a donut?" And I-I wanted a donut. And now it’s five years later, the donut’s gone and I’m still Toby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Five years?! Chandler you have to tell him!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! That would be so awkward! Look—Besides, we work in different departments. He’s on the sixth floor y’know? So he calls me Toby once in a while. What’s the big deal? It could be worse, it’s not like he’s calling me Muriel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Muriel. Wh-why would he call you Muriel? Oh my God! Chandler M Bing? It’s not just an M, your middle name is Muriel!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Shh! It is a family name!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler Muriel Bing. Boy, your parents never gave you a chance did they?

lines.speaker: Joey's Co-Star
lines.text: Drake, I’ve discovered the reason for all your headaches and memory loss.

lines.speaker: Dr. Drake Ramoray
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Joey's Co-Star
lines.text: Apparently your brain transplant was not entirely successful. It seems your body is rejecting Jessica’s brain.

lines.speaker: Dr. Drake Ramoray
lines.text: Is it serious?

lines.speaker: Joey's Co-Star
lines.text: Not if we extract tissue from the original host body, synthesize antibodies, and introduce them into your system, which could stop it from rejecting the brain.

lines.speaker: Dr. Drake Ramoray
lines.text: Well that sounds simple enough, let’s just do that.

lines.speaker: Joey's Co-Star
lines.text: We would, but when we went to exhume Jessica’s body, it was gone.

lines.text: Cut! Very nice people!

lines.text: Okay, let me just get changed and we can go to dinner.

lines.text: Well don’t—What happened to Jessica’s body?!

lines.text: I’m not telling, you’ll have to see it on TV!

lines.text: You don’t know do you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey you too!

lines.speaker: Joey's Co-Star
lines.text: Alright.

lines.text: What? You weren’t in it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh sorry. Uh-uh, Kash?

lines.text: Yes?

lines.text: This is my friend Rachel. Rachel, Kash, Kash, Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.text: Hey! How come I haven’t seen you here before?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, Joey probably thinks I’ll just embarrass him. Y’know, he thinks I’m some kind of a soap opera nut—Which I’m not! I’m not. Although I do know that your uh, your favorite ice cream flavor is butter pecan. And uh, and that your-your dog’s name is Wally. Well look at that, I’m just stroking your arm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here we go! Here we go!
lines.stage_direction: grabbing her Starts to pull her away from Kash.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, we’re leaving. Bye Kash.

lines.speaker: Kash
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Say hi to Wally.

lines.text: Ooh Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! I had the best time with Tim last night. He is so sweet! Oh, I can’t wait to get sous-neath him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I…I have to fire him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But why?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because he’s terrible! Okay, he’s slow, he burns things, last night he lit my pastry chef on fire!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well maybe he was just nervous, y’know you can be very intimidating. And besides I’ve met your pastry chef and she can stand to be taken down a peg or two.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, now she has no eyebrows, mission accomplished.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But Monica, he loves his job so much! Can you just give him another chance? Please?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, but if-if he lights someone else on fire he is out of there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s fair! Thank you so much. Thanks. Oops, it looks like when he got the pastry chef he got you a little bit too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I paid to have this done.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Love it!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: Oh you know uh Kash, really liked you the other day. He said he thought you were charming.

lines.text: I thought I was a complete idiot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I’m with you. He even asked me if I thought you’d go out with him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh, I think I’m gonna throw up a little bit. What did you say?

lines.text: I said no.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? I…I just figured since you’re pregnant you’re not gonna be seeing people.

lines.text: Okay Joey, first of all Kash Ford is not people. Second of all, what did he say when you told him I was pregnant?

lines.text: I didn’t tell him. I didn’t know if you were telling people. This is back when I thought Kash was still people.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good-good, don’t tell him. Don’t tell him. Just have him call me okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach look, I really don’t think that’s such a great…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you go do it! I’ll come back to that set! I’ll meet more actors! I’ll meet ‘em all!

lines.text: Hey Bob.

lines.text: If I see him, I’ll ask.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Toby!

lines.speaker: Mr. Franklin
lines.text: Hey-hey! Bing? Was that Bob from six you were just talking too?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Mr. Franklin
lines.text: Oh then you know each other.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We’re on a semi-first name basis.

lines.text: What do you think of adding him to our team?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bob? Ooh, working here with us? Everyday? Yeah, I don’t know if he has what it takes.

lines.text: Really? They love him down on six.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But this is eleven. It’s almost twice as hard up here.

lines.speaker: Mr. Franklin
lines.text: Okay, I hear you loud and clear. Bob will stay put.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think it’s best sir.

lines.speaker: Mr. Franklin
lines.text: But we really do need to find someone up here. The work is starting to pile up. I’ve got a stack of documents on my desk this high.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know what you should do, just toss ‘em in the shedder and claim you never got ‘em.

lines.text: That’s a good one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What does a guy have to do to be taken seriously around here?!
