lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey. Okay, I gave him another chance, but Tim has got to go!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No-no! He is totally incompetent. I called the chef who recommended him to me. He said, "Ha-ha! Gotcha!"

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Okay, but you can’t fire him today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because I’m dumping him today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! You said he was sweet!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He is sweet. He’s too sweet. He calls me all the time. "So did-did you get home from work okay?" "Did-did you get out of the shower okay?"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just don’t pick up your phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then he comes over! "I’m so worried about you." Uck! Be a man!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? So now I’m not allowed to fire him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You can’t fire him and dump him the same day, he’ll kill himself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay well then, I’ll fire him today and you go out with him for another week.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you kidding?! Another week with that sip, I’ll kill my self!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay well, then we’ll both do it today and he’ll just have to deal with it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. But the question is who’s gonna go first. ‘Cause whoever goes second is the bitch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on! The boss that fires a guy that’s just been dumped, bitch! And the woman who dumps a guy that’s just been fired, blond bitch!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wanted to do this days ago so I think I should go first.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, that makes sense. Ugh. But—Screw you I’m going first!
lines.stage_direction: Starts towards the door. She grabs her purse and runs out.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is in the kitchen as Phoebe enters.

lines.text: Hey Toby, you got a sec?

lines.text: Sure, what’s up?

lines.text: I just had a meeting, I was actually hoping to get transferred up here, but I just found out its not gonna happen. Apparently somebody thinks I’m not eleventh floor material. Say uh, who the hell is this Chandler?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, what do you think is a better excuse for why I’m not drinking on this date tonight. "Umm, I’m a recovering alcoholic. I’m a Mormon," or "I got so hammered last night I’m still a little drunk?"

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, what do you want to do tonight? There’s a Ukrainian film at the Angelica that’s supposed to be very powerful. Interested?

lines.text: No. No. But I’ll go see a normal person movie with ya.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach? You wanna come?

lines.text: Oh no, I can’t. I got a date.

lines.text: It’s no Ukrainian film.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah—Ooh! Earrings!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A date?! She’s-she’s got a date?! With who?

lines.text: I set her up with this actor on my show.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You set her up?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey what-what were you thinking?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I was thinking that it’d probably be okay because Ross hasn’t gone out with Rachel in five years!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, I’m not worried about her! I’m worried about my baby! Whoever she dates my baby dates! Now-now where is this actor taking them?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! I’m an actor too! I’m not sure. I think they’re taking the ferry out to some Italian place on Staten Island.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A ferry? My baby is going on a ferry? Do you have any idea how dangerous those are?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are we talking about one of those big boats that carry cars that go like five miles an hour?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why don’t they just jump out of an airplane?! Huh?! That-that’s a fun date! Or burn each other with matches?! That’s fun too! Whew!!

lines.speaker: Kash
lines.text: Hey Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Kash. Uh hey-hey this is Ross. Ross, this is Kash.

lines.text: Hey.

lines.text: Hi. I-I hear you’re going on a ferry tonight.

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.text: Hey guys do you think this is too slutty—Hi Kash!

lines.text: Okay. Have a great time you guys.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Kash
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes it is too slutty!
lines.stage_direction: Joey slams the door before Rachel could hear the entire sentence.

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door and Joey answers it.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Hi!
lines.stage_direction: walks behind Phoebe And startles her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Oh, I’m so glad you called. I feel like it’s always me calling you. So, what’s up? Is everything okay with Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It will be…in a minute. Listen, Tim you’re a really great guy.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: It’s because I’m with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Aw. I’m just—I’m in a place in my life right now where I…I…
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe gets a bad taste in her mouth when he looks away

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is reading a magazine as Tim enters.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Whoops. Sorry. This just in. Oh, it’s 9-1-1 from the restaurant, that means Monica needs me right away.
lines.stage_direction: Looking at it, like a newscaster Phoebe fake laughs. Starts to leave.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh-oh no she doesn’t! I know what that is. You can stay.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Awww, I’ll miss you too Pheebs. And I will be holding you, right here.
lines.stage_direction: Starts to leave Holds his hands over his heart, blows a kiss to Phoebe who catches it, and then leaves and Phoebe throws the kiss back.

scenes.scene_description: Tim’s beeper goes off and he answers it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what movie do you want to see—And not another one I have to read. Okay? I get enough of that from books.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Books?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, car magazines, cereal boxes, but it’s like enough !

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I tell you what. Why don’t we uh, why don’t we just stay here? Let’s not see a movie, we’ll just hang.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And just wait for Rachel to come back from her date?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, if that’s what you want to do I’m not gonna say no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude! What is going on?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just…I have to find out how it went.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This guy could be my baby’s stepfather!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They go on one date and you’re worried about them getting married?! He’s not you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just—I-I can’t believe she’s-she’s dating?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well Ross, what did you think she was gonna do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t know! I guess I just can’t believe any of this is happening.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s just I always thought when I had another kid it would be different. Now I-I love Ben, but every time I have to drop him off at Carol and Susan’s, it’s like—It breaks my heart a little. I mean I’ve always had this picture of me and my next wife in bed on Sunday and, my kid comes running in and leaps up onto the bed. And we all read the paper together. Y’know? Maybe fight over the science section.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s a nice picture. Maybe you can still have that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No I can’t. I mean Rachel’s out with some guy. My baby went with her. If anything that picture keeps moving further away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, can I ask you something? In this, in this picture of you and your wife, is your wife Rachel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It used to be. Now she doesn’t really have a face. Smokin’ body though.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good call. Yeah. But, the face Ross, the face isn’t Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No but ahh! How much easier would it be if it were?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, but I don’t think that’s what she wants.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it’s not what I want either. I mean I-I can’t force myself to fall in love with her again now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s okay Ross maybe you need a new picture. Okay? It’s not gonna be what you thought, but no matter what there’s gonna be a brand new little baby, your baby. Who cares what the picture looks like?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I tell you what. Let’s you and me go out and have some fun. Huh? Whatever you want. Come on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We can still catch that Ukrainian film.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I said fun!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Ross are having dinner.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hey Toby!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Bobby.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: It’s Bob actually. Hey, you work up here, can you tell me where this Chandler Bing ’s office is?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhh yeah. Yeah, it’s right, right down there. Right there, yeah. Can I ask you why?
lines.stage_direction: Points down the hall When he has Bob looking down the hall, he turns around and knocks his nameplate off of his door.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I want to talk to that bastard, see what his problem is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay Bob listen uhh, I’m the reason you didn’t get the job up here.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Toby don’t.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bob!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Toby! I’m not gonna let you cover for him. Anything you say right now will just get me more upset with Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that puts me in a difficult position.

scenes.scene_description: Outside Chandler’s Office, Chandler is just about to go into his office when Bob calls for him from behind.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: I got your page, is everything okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh well that depends, how are things with Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Oh it’s great! It’s great! Thank you so much for introducing us!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my pleasure. Okay, I’m afraid I’ve got some bad news. Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re fired!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m breaking up with you!

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m, I’m breaking up with you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re fired.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m sorry, I’m just—I’m…I’m just not ready for a relationship right now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah and-and I’m sorry too. But, well I just—I like things done a certain way and the chemistry’s just not right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that’s good, the chemistry thing for us too.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Wow! Umm, okay. Umm… I-I-I realize I came on a little strong but, it’s only because I think you’re so amazing. And uh, I-I just wanna, I just wanna tell you how much I appreciate you giving me an opportunity here because I—you’re the most talented chef I’ve ever worked for. Anyway…

scenes.scene_description: Monica’s Restaurant Kitchen, Monica is waiting for Tim who enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Tim wait!

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, I think I spoke too quickly. There-there’s a learning curve with this job and maybe we can try it again.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Thank you so much! ‘Cause I-I know I can do better!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Starts to leave and Phoebe and Monica trade looks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And Tim I just wanna say, good luck here.
lines.stage_direction: Shakes his hand and leaves, which disgusts Monica.

scenes.scene_description: They both look at Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, how was the date?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well I’m alone and I just bought fifteen dollars worth of candy bars, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh. What happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I made the mistake of telling him that I was pregnant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, he didn’t uh, take it so well?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well better than you, but y’know still not what you want.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He got all weird and sputtery and then he said uh, "Yeah, I hear those hemorrhoids are a bitch."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He sounds swell.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Doesn’t he?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, wanna…wanna a little cheering up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sit down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: A Street, Ross is walking past a newsstand and sees Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Guess whose middle name is Muriel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler M. Bing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah-ha!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m sorry about your date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh it’s all right. I’m guess I’m just done with the whole dating thing. It’s one more thing in my life that’s suddenly completely different. This is hard.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah I know. On the other hand in um, in about seven months you’re gonna have something that you’re gonna love more than any guy you’ve ever gone out with. Just wait. Wait until uh, wait until the first time your baby grabs your finger. You have no idea.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thanks sweetie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You wanna, you wanna grab some coffee?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, I think I’m gonna go home and eat ten candy bars.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I thought I cheered you up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh you did, there are twenty in here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right. Good night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good night.

scenes.scene_description: They sit on some front steps.
He kisses her on the cheek and heads to Central Perk.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hey it’s Mona! From the wedding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hi!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Uh…how are you?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I’m good except umm, you still owe me a dance.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh that’s right. Well uh, would you be interested in seeing a Ukrainian film?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh you’re serious. Sure!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great! Well umm…

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Ross is entering and Mona from the wedding recognizes him.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I think I might need one more cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure! Uh, let me get it for ya.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Ross continues to talk to her as the camera pans to show Rachel standing in the doorway.
Rachel watches that and slowly backs out to head for home.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bob. Bob! Bob!!! What the hell are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I just found out this is Chandler’s office! Come on Toby, give me a hand!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler’s Office, Bob is ransacking Chandler’s office.
He thinks about it and decides to join in by turning over a chair and continue ransacking the place.

title: The One With The Halloween Party

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey you guys?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know it’s last minute, but we decided to have a Halloween party.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh good!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And everybody has to wear costumes. Come on! It’ll be fun!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I’ll-I’ll be there. I mean I have to wear a costume to all my classes that day anyway so…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Please tell me you’re not gonna dress up like a dinosaur.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not two years in a row.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I’ll come to the party but I’m not dressing up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You have to!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No way! Look, Halloween is so stupid! Dressing up, pretending to be someone you’re not…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’re an actor!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So Ross, are you gonna bring Mona?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, I think I will.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That hot girl from their wedding?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well hey-hey if she needs any idea for costumes, she could be a bikini model, or a slutty nurse, or a sexy cheerleader huh—Ooh-ooh, Leatherface from The Texas Chainsaw Massacre —No-no-no! Slutty Leatherface.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now wasn’t Joey hitting on her at the wedding too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s right! He was hitting on her, and I got her. I guess the better man won. Please don’t take her from me.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, everyone is there as Chandler and Monica enter. Oh, and Joey is wearing a FDNY T-shirt to make this the first nod to the tragedy that Friends have made.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ursula! Wait! Err-err, it’s me! Phoebe!
lines.stage_direction: Ursula turns, smiles, and continues walking.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Oh, I thought there was a mirror there. Okay, bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait a second! So, what’s new with you?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Umm, nothing. I mean, I’m getting married next week.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah, it’s gonna be a small ceremony. Just family. His.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh. Okay. Well, I’m really happy for you.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Wait! If umm, if you want to come, I guess that’d be okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Sure! Why not? You could be my sister for the day.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Okay. Umm, y’know, my friends are having a Halloween party tonight at my old apartment so, you could come. Maybe I could meet the guy you’re marrying.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Huh. Well, I’m supposed to be working at the restaurant tonight. I’m supposed to be working right now, so who cares.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: By the way, it’s a costume party.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Oh! Okay, so that’s why you’re…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. But thanks.

scenes.scene_description: A Street, Phoebe is walking down it and passes Ursula.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait! You’re supposed to wear a costume!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am! I am a woman who spent a lot of money on a dress and she wants to wear it, because soon she won’t be able to fit into it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m Catwoman, who wants to borrow the dress when you’re too big for it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, The Halloween party has started. Monica is setting out some food as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Kids
lines.text: Trick or treat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh! Can I give out the candy? I really want to be with the kids right now. Y’know, ever since I got pregnant I-I have the strongest maternal instincts.

lines.speaker: Kids
lines.text: Trick or treat!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just a minute!!! Look at you guys! Wow! You are a very scary witch.
lines.stage_direction: to them She takes the candy and opens the door to two parents, a witch, a clown, and a cowgirl. Gives her candy.

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And you are a very funny clown.

lines.speaker: Clown
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And you are so in style right now. Y’know, I work at Ralph Lauren and the whole fall line has got this like equestrian theme going on. I don’t suppose you saw the cover of British Vogue , but…

lines.speaker: Cowgirl
lines.text: Can I just have the candy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Sure.
lines.stage_direction: Throws some in her bag and she walks away as Phoebe, dressed as Supergirl walks up and eyes Monica who eyes her back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah, Catwoman. So we meet again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So we do Supergirl.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it’s me. Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica! Can I talk to you for a second? Listen, I appreciate you getting me the costume…
lines.stage_direction: entering from the bedroom wearing a big, pink bunny costume

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you did this to him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! I thought he’d love it! His favorite kid's book was the Velveteen Rabbit !

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The Velveteen Rabbit was brown and white!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, it was either a pink bunny or no bunny at all.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No bunny at all!! Always no bunny at all!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You didn’t dress up either?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes I did! I’m Chandler. Dude, what happened?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How is that me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. I’m Chandler
lines.stage_direction: makes a growling/gurgling sound at the end and the girls laugh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is so you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When have I ever done that?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: When have I ever done that?!

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Trick or treat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Well you’re just the prettiest ballerina I’ve ever seen.
lines.stage_direction: Opens the door to reveal a ballerina

lines.speaker: Ballerina
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow! That deserves another piece of candy.

lines.speaker: Ballerina
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I have to say that earns tutu pieces of candy.

lines.speaker: Ballerina
lines.text: I love you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh… Oh, honey here. Take it all. Monica! We need more candy?
lines.stage_direction: Pours the entire large bowl into her bag and closes the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! There’s only been like four kids.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I know, but one of them just said that she loved me so I just gave her everything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No wonder your pregnant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!
lines.stage_direction: entering He’s wearing a costume as well.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you supposed to be?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Remember the Russian satellite, Sputnik? Well, I’m a potato or a…spud. And these are my antennae. So Sputnik, becomes… Spud-nik. Spudnik!
lines.stage_direction: They all look at him. Points to the colander with an old TV antenna glued on top that he’s wearing. They’re still confused

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! I don’t have the worst costume anymore!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey all right, Ross came as doody.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I-I’m not doody.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, space doody!

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Aren’t you gonna give me a kiss?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I will. But right after you tell me who the hell you are.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Ursula?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ursula’s fianc�e?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh my God, you’re the sister!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Okay, I just slapped my future sister-in-law’s ass.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I’m an idiot. Uh, is your mother here? Maybe I can give her a little slap on the butt.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My mother killed herself.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: She, now I knew that and…now I’m sweating. Look at me, I’m really sweating—Now I’m saying, "Look at me," I’m getting even sweatier. I think I probably should go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no! That’s okay, we’ll just start over. Okay? Hi! I’m Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Eric.
lines.stage_direction: They shake hands and he’s squinting. And, no, it’s not me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why are you looking at me like that?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: ‘Cause the sweat’s getting in my eyes and its burning.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. So, what are you?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I don’t think they have a name for it. It’s just I get nervous; I start sweating like crazy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I-I meant your costume.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh umm, I’m the solar system. Yeah, my students helped me make it—I teach the second grade.
lines.stage_direction: He’s wearing a black sweater with the planets glued on around the sun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love the second grade!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! It’s so much better than first grade when you don’t know what’s going on and definitely better than third grade. Y’know with all the politics and mind games.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: So what do you do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, I’m a masseuse…by day.
lines.stage_direction: Stands with her hands on her hips like a Supergirl pose.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Y’know you don’t have to stand here with me, believe me…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I’m having fun. I’m really—And I’m really-really excited for you and Ursula.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh I feel very lucky, she’s great. I think she’s the most beautiful woman I’ve ever seen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You read comic books right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Exclusively.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who do you think would win in a fight, Catwoman or Supergirl?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Catwoman, hands down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But between you and Phoebe, I’d have to give the edge to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Really?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kiddin’? Phoebe lived on the street. Okay? Plus, she’s got this crazy temper. She—She’s not standing right behind me is she?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No you’re fine. All right well, do you think I could take Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m not sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Come on I am tough! Punch me right here! As hard as you can!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Will you relax?! What are you taking this so seriously for? It doesn’t matter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh really? Okay? Well what would you say if I told you that, y’know, Ross or Chandler could beat you up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I would say, "Woman, please!"

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey. Ursula’s fianc�e is really sweet! He’s a teacher, he does all this volunteer work. Y’know normally y’know, I don’t like really sweaty guys. But this one? I could just mop him up!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re getting a crush on your sister’s fianc�e.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I’m not! You are!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here comes the temper.

scenes.scene_description: Joey gives him the okay symbol, and Ross rushes towards him to be stopped by Chandler. Meanwhile, Phoebe goes over to the snack table as some guy, which turns out to be Ursula's fianc�e Eric, walks in and smacks her butt.

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Trick or treat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Y’know what honey, we’re actually out of candy right now. But someone just went out to get some and I have been giving out money but I’m out of that too. Hey, can I write you a check?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, what’s your name?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Lelani Mayolanofavich.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I’m just gonna write this out to cash.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Mona!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! Hi!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey’s gonna be thrilled! He was hoping you’d come by as a slutty nurse.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Umm, actually I’m just a nurse.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’d think that would embarrass me, but you see I’m maxed out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You made it!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Wait-wait! You’re umm, you’re a potato…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I’m a spud…

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: And the antennae…Oh my God you’re Spudnik!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Marry her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, here’s a good one for ya. Who do think would win in a fight between Ross and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can’t answer that! Chandler’s my husband.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So Ross?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Hey beautiful.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello handsome. Oh God. Oh look at you two. So when did you guys meet?
lines.stage_direction: Sees Ursula over her shoulder Walks away in shame as they kiss.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Two weeks ago.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Two weeks? That’s it?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Yeah, I know it sounds crazy, and it’s not like me to do something so impulsive, but she’s just so perfect, and we have so much in common.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh really?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: We’re both teachers.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh?
lines.stage_direction: Ursula motions for Phoebe to keep quiet.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: And we were both in the Peace Corps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Peace Corps, really?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: In fact when we were building houses in Uruguay, we were, we were just two towns apart and we never met.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah. It wasn’t a town when I got there, but it was a town when I left. Shall we get me really drunk?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Sure.

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door and Rachel opens it to a little girl.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Howdy doody.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s funny. Yeah. Y’know you’re the funniest man here in a pink bunny costume his wife made him wear.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh relax man, relax. You’re looking a little flushed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey, I think we might find out the answer to our question.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What question?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica and I were talking about who could kick whose ass in a fight, you or Ross?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There’s no question.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you think Ross too?

scenes.scene_description: They walk away.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross is really strong! Okay, he’s the strongest out of all three of you! Except for Joey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I cannot believe you didn’t pick me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, in her defense, she’s right. I am stronger. I would destroy you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh really?! You think you’re stronger? Why don’t you prove it?
lines.stage_direction: He pushes Ross who starts to fall backwards until Mona catches him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh I’ll prove it! I’ll prove it like a theorem!!

scenes.scene_description: Monica turns around slowly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait-wait!! Okay, stop it! Stop it! Stop! Now listen, no one’s gonna fight in this apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Monica! People came to see a fight, let’s give ‘em what they came for!
lines.stage_direction: Grabs her and pulls her into the living room.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hey, you guys could arm wrestle.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Listen to the slutty nurse.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’re going down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah? You’re going further down! Downtown!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Seriously guys, the trash talk is embarrassing.

scenes.scene_description: They start to fight with Ross pulling on Chandler’s ears and Chandler hitting Ross over the head with his carrot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Gunther! You brought candy! Thank you so much for picking this up! You are so sweet.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, someday you are gonna make some man the luckiest guy in the world.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, the party continues with Rachel leaning on the counter as Gunther walks in carrying candy.

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.text: Trick or treat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gotta go! Hi! Wow! There you go!
lines.stage_direction: Opens the door to a boy in a cape.

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: My friend Lewis told me you were giving out money.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, we were but umm, now we’ve got candy.

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: I’d rather have the money.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that-that’s not your choice. Happy Halloween!

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: This isn’t fair.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well is it fair that all you did was put on a cape and I gotta give you free stuff?

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: Shut up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You shut up!

scenes.scene_description: Hands him some candy.

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: You can’t tell me to shut up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, I think I just did. And uh-oh, here it comes again. Shut up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I know—I’m good—I got it! Now wait a minute, I’ve got one more thing I have to say to you…oh right! Shut up!
lines.stage_direction: To Joey Joey slowly backs away. To the boy

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: You’re a mean old woman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Wait no! Shut up—I mean don’t cry! Let me get my checkbook!
lines.stage_direction: Grabs her checkbook and runs after him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look honey, you don’t have to do this, okay? It’s the strength you have inside that means the most to me. You’re loyal, you’re honest, and you have integrity! That’s the kind of strength that I want in the man that I love!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That means nothing to me. Come on!

scenes.scene_description: The gang gets interested now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi liar!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know the only reason he’s marrying you is because he thinks all the things you were saying about yourself were true.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Well they could be true.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But they’re not!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, it’s a fine line huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why are you lying to him?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: I don’t know. He said he did all this stuff and then I said I did it too and he got so excited, it was really fun.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Honey?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: It’s a filthy, disgusting habit and I want you to quit now!
lines.stage_direction: waving the cigarette in Phoebe’s face

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: She’s helped so many people to quit smoking.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Y’know, we’d really better get going.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh right, you’ve got a church group meeting tonight.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Well, it was nice meeting you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You too. And Ursula?! It was really nice meeting you tonight!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs come on! Bunny vs. Doody! We’re waiting! Okay. Okay guys, one match, winner take all. Oh wait-wait! What does the winner get?
lines.stage_direction: entering They go inside. To Chandler and Ross. They grasp each other’s hand in preparation for battle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pride.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And dignity.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, if you say so. All right, ready? Set! Go!

scenes.scene_description: The Hallway, Phoebe is exiting Monica and Chandler’s, and finds Ursula standing in the hallway smoking.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Wow! They’re both really strong.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or equally weak.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmm?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler’s making his sex face.

scenes.scene_description: They start wrestling, only they are unable to move either one’s arm despite a huge strain on their faces and a cheering crowd.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, you gettin’ tired?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope! I can do this all day.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Me too. Gettin’ a little tired though.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: God, I’m exhausted.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look this is starting to look really bad for me. Okay? Mona, Mona’s standing right over there. Oh God, she’s talking to Joey! You gotta let me win!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No way! If anything you’ve gotta let me win! My wife thinks I’m a wimp!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, at least you have a wife! I-I keep getting divorces and knockin’ people up! And I’m dressed as doody.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’re Spudnik.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, who are we kidding? I’m doody. Please? She’s watchin’.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine. Oh no!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yay! My hero!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You’re a weird lady.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Hey. Ursula said she left her purse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: What a relief. It has all the numbers of the people in her prayer chain.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure it does. Yeah, yeah.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Well, I guess I’ll see you at the wedding.
lines.stage_direction: Exits and Phoebe follows him into the hall.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm listen, I don’t think…I don’t think I’m gonna make it to the wedding. So I just want to wish you all the luck in the world.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I think we’ll be okay. Besides it’s so perfect and she’s been saving herself for me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay I can’t let you do this! She’s lying to you.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She is lying! And I bet I can prove it. Excuse me. Okay. Okay. Yeah—Not a prayer chain, but what looks like a detailed drawing of a bank floor plan. Okay, here’s the nametag from the restaurant where she works as a waitress! Not a teacher, a waitress. All right, here’s her driver license, this oughta be good, she always lies about this. How old did she say she was?
lines.stage_direction: She grabs Ursula’s purse and starts going through it and finds some papers. Holds up her nametag.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: She told me she was 25.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I almost don’t want to show this. Just remember I’m a minute younger.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I am so stupid. Of course she was lying! She’s not a teacher. There’s not such a thing as the top secret elementary school for the children of spies.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. You’re not, you’re not stupid.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I’m not smart. I just wanted so much to…be impulsive once. To be romantic.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s good, you should be impulsive and you should be romantic. Just…you did it with the wrong person. What?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: It’s just so weird, two people look so much alike, and so different.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Eric!! Let’s go!!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I’d better go, deal…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you should.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Hurry up I gotta pray!!

scenes.scene_description: Basically Chandler’s face looks like he’s not all there and is staring off into the distance…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Well, I had to give the kid fifty bucks to stop crying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s not so bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I also had to go to a couple houses with him as his girlfriend. Oh, I am just awful with children!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! You’re good with kids. They’re just crazy on Halloween. Y’know, they’re all greedy and hopped up on sugar!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really? You think that’s all it is?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Absolutely! Halloween is the worst. Except for Christmas…and their birthdays. Kinda get a little crazy during the summer too. And anytime they’re hungry or sleepy. Y’know, kids are tough. Good luck with that.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe hands Eric Ursula’s purse and he walks away.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, I wanted to tell I’m-I’m sorry you lost.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen, I’ve got a secret for ya. I let him win.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that a secret or a lie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I let him win—Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Would you tell her I let you win please?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Yeah. Uh Chandler let me win. No, Chandler’s really strong. Oh my arm is so sore. Oh nurse!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am strong! I’ll show you!
lines.stage_direction: He sits down at the table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler please!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh what’s the matter? Are you scared?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let’s go big bunny!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Chandler and Monica are standing in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. 1…2…3—Go! I’m gonna kill myself!
lines.stage_direction: Once again he’s at a stalemate, but this time he’s in pain. Pause

scenes.scene_description: They assume the starting position.

title: The One With The Stain

lines.speaker: Dutch Phrases by
lines.text: Kenny Walgraef

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is sitting in the living room as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! You cleaned! Look at these floors! You did the windows! Oh, I have been begging you for months and you did! You cleaned! And nagging works!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know uh, I didn’t actually do this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, was I cleaning in my sleep again?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, it wasn’t you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well then who?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got a maid. Yay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I hope by maid you mean mistress, because if some other woman was here cleaning then…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh honey, I know you don’t like to relinquish control…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, relinquish is just a fancy word for lose!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, she’s really nice. Okay? And she mentioned that she adored the way that you arranged the sponges.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did she really say that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, I distinctly remember ‘cause I thought it was a joke. Now just give her a chance, okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine, I can do it. Whew.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What’s the matter?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, usually when I’m this anxious, I clean!

scenes.scene_description: Monica notices something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who’s cell phone is that? It’s just so annoying; everywhere you go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think it’s coming from your bag.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I never get calls!! Hello?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Hi, it’s Eric. From the Halloween party, Ursula’s fianc�e.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God Eric hi! Wait, how’d you get this number?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh, I have a friend who’s a cop and he got it for me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! What an incredible violation—and wonderful surprise.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Uh listen, I just—I thought you should know I broke up with Ursula.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh you did? He did it! He did it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! What did he do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shhh! I’m talking.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Anyway, I was wondering if, you were the sort of person who…eats lunch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you asking me out? ‘Cause it would be kinda weird since you just broke up with my sister.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Yeah uh…okay. I’m-I’m sorry. Bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Wait! I was just saying that so you’d think I was a good person. Fight for me.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Uhh, I won’t take no for an answer.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not great, but we can work on it at lunch. Okay, I can be at your apartment in two hours.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Great! But wh-wh—How do you know where I live?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’ve got friends too. Okay, bye.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Bye.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe are there as someone’s cell phone starts to ring with one of those fancy ring tones.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! I’m going out with Eric! Ooh, this day is really gonna be so much better than I thought it was gonna be. Oh Ross, I can’t make lunch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So apparently I’m available for lunch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can’t. I’m busy. I’m apartment hunting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’re moving?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I can’t live with Joey once the baby comes. I don’t want my child’s first words to be, "How you doin’?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So does-does Joey know you’re moving?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I haven’t discussed it with him yet, but I know he’s gonna be relieved. Last week, he brought this girl over and I started talking to her about morning sickness and then I showed her pictures from my pregnancy book.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s not really porn.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Not so much.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y’know what and if you’re looking for a place? I just heard in the elevator this morning that a woman in my building died.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Was she old? Does she have a view?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I don’t know, but how-how great would that be huh? You living in my building. I could help take care of the baby. I can come over whenever I want. With your permission.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah that would really be great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well can we see it?! Oh maybe we shouldn’t. I mean if she just died this morning out of respect.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. No. No you’re right.

scenes.scene_description: She hangs up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shall we?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

scenes.scene_description: Pause as they both take another sip of coffee.
They both exit.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. I’m Ross Geller. I live in the building.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I’m Rachel, an admirer of the building.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I heard about Mrs. Verhoeven passing away and I’m so sorry for your loss.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: She didn’t pass.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: My mother’s still alive.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thank God!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: It looked like we were gonna lose her this morning, but she’s a tough old bird.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahh.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: Are you close with her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Of course! Uh yeah, she and I would talk all the time in-in the laundry room.
lines.stage_direction: Rachel pokes her head in and starts to look around Pushes Rachel out of the way.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: You speak Dutch? Zeer vereerd een vriend

lines.speaker: van mijn moeder te ontmoeten. (Translation
lines.text: I’m very honored to meet a friend of my mother.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know I would it’s just painful.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So she’s really not dead.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Verhoeven's Daughter
lines.text: No, she’s hanging in there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm. Do you think—Could you tell me if she’s hanging in, in a one bedroom or a two?

scenes.scene_description: Ross’s Building, they are approaching the apartment of the woman who died. Ross knocks on the door and a woman answers it.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: Mrs. Bing, this tile cleaner is incredible! Where’d you get it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh well umm, I make it myself! It’s two parts ammonia and one part lemon juice. And now the secret ingredient is…y’know what? We just met.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: Okay. Uhh, I’m gonna go get the clothes from the laundry room now. And, when I come back I’ll clean behind the refrigerator.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love her.

lines.speaker: Brenda
lines.text: I’ll be back in a minute.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: As Brenda exits Monica notices something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See? I told you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She stole my jeans!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I have been looking for them all week and she is wearing them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So she stole your pants and then she came back and wore them in front of you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don’t you see? It’s the perfect crime!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She must’ve been planning this for years!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I will prove it to you! Okay? About a week ago I was wearing those jeans and I dropped a pen in my lap and it left an ink stain on the crotch. Now when she comes back I will find it and show you that stain!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, isn’t it possible that the company that sold the jeans made more than just the one pair?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, shouldn’t we go give her the benefit of the doubt before we go…snooping around her crotch?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine. I’m just glad I didn’t give her my secret ingredient.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Out of curiosity, what is your secret ingredient?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is observing the new maid, Brenda, clean.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Eric

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.text: Just seeing her brought it all back. All the lies, the way she used me. I just…I got so angry just looking at her……face.

lines.text: Yeah. Yeah.

lines.text: I’m sorry. I just…when I look at you I see her. When I see her I get a little bit angry.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: No wait! There’s only a problem when I look at you. Oh I got it! I got it.

lines.text: No don’t tear out your eyes!!

lines.text: I was just, I was just gonna take out my lenses.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah try that. So, is that better?
lines.stage_direction: He finishes and looks at her.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Not really. You…you’re blurry, but you still look like Ursula. You’re Blursula. Okay wait. Maybe…If I-if I just don’t look at you for a while. See? It…it works. I’m not, I’m not angry at all anymore! This is a great date!
lines.stage_direction: Stands up and turns his back to her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look Eric, turn around. Look, I like you, but it shouldn’t be this hard. Y’know? This is our first date y’know? First dates are supposed to be about excitement and electricity and ‘Ooh, he just touched my hand, did he mean to touch my hand?’ and y’know first kisses and……second kisses.
lines.stage_direction: He does so. He kisses her Motions for him to kiss her again which he does and they start to make out.
