lines.text: Hi. Listen, I’m sorry about that whole thing with Roger. It really wasn’t right, and I, and I want to make it up to you, so umm, I brought you something that I think you’ll really enjoy. Now, this is just a loan. Okay? I’m gonna, I’m gonna want him back. So… I’m gonna go now. I’m sorry, I thought I could do it and I can’t!
lines.stage_direction: Goes into the hallway and returns carrying the Evander Holyfield cutout. Looks at him longingly Exits. Pause Entering She grabs the cutout and exits for good.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is there as Phoebe enters.

title: The One Where Joey Dates Rachel

lines.speaker: Directed by
lines.text: David Schwimmer

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! I got you a present!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my goodness! Where did you hide it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I got it for your wedding and I ordered it weeks ago, and it finally got here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs you didn’t have to get us anything for our wedding you already sang…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love it! It’s huge!! Let’s open it! Open it!!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are entering carrying groceries and find Phoebe already there standing in front of a huge object that has been gift wrapped.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s a Ms. Pac-Man machine!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I didn’t know where to put it so I just left it here for now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh well, maybe we can put it in the guest bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right.
lines.stage_direction: He goes to push it and it doesn’t move.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I kinda like it here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do you really like it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you kidding?! I practically spent my entire childhood at the arcade! This is like—Oh my, this is like my second favorite game!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh really? What was your first?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well I-I really don’t remember the name of it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well what did it do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you just—you put a quarter in and y’know pull-pull some handles and win like a candy bar or something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A vending machine?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don’t feel bad for me! I won like every time!

scenes.scene_description: Monica rips open the paper.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Oh, I’m so glad you guys are here. I’ve been dying to tell someone what happened in the Paleontology department today.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you think he saw us or can we still sneak out?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Professor Neuman, the head of the department, so….

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They made you head of the department!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I get to teach one of his advanced classes! Why didn’t I get head of the department?
lines.stage_direction: Pause Goes and gets some coffee.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Hey Rach, listen umm…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I got a big date coming up, do you know a good restaurant?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, Paul’s Caf�. They got great food and it’s really romantic.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh, great! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Oh, and then afterwards you can take her to the Four Seasons for drinks. Or you go downtown and listen to some jazz. Or dancing—Oh! Take her dancing!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You sure are naming a lot of ways to postpone sex, I’ll tell ya…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I miss dating. Gettin’ all dressed up and going to a fancy restaurant. I’m not gonna be able to do that for so long, and it’s so much fun! I mean not that sitting at home worrying about giving birth to a sixteen pound baby is not fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, y’know what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why don’t I take you out?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! Joey, you don’t want to go on a date with a pregnant lady.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes I do! And we’re gonna go out, we’re gonna have a good time, and take your mind off of childbirth and c-sections and-and giant baby heads stretching out…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! I’ll go with ya! I’ll go! I’ll go with ya.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’ll be fun.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey and Rachel are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. No! Yes! Ahh. Oh, would you look at that Monica? I just knocked off all of your top scores, how sad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I’m next. Don’t! Don’t start another game! I said I’m next! Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I’m sorry. I didn’t hear you over all the winning.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler! Phoebe’s hogging the game!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who cares? It’s a stupid game.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You only think it’s stupid because you suck at it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t suck. It’s sucks. You suck.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait okay, if this game is gonna cause problems between the two of you, then maybe I should just keep it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No-no! I love it! It is a great present! In fact, why don’t you go home and wait for the thank you card?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why do you want to play this game so bad?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! It’s not like it spits out a Clark bar after every game.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Phoebe that’s it. Come on, get out—out of the chair. Get out! Oh come…Phoebe!
lines.stage_direction: She goes to move Phoebe, but Phoebe goes limp and Monica can’t move her.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is watching Phoebe play Ms. Pac-Man .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey? Could you get that? What are you doing here? I thought you were in your room?
lines.stage_direction: There is no answer and she goes and opens the door to Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I’m picking you up for our date. These are for you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, Lilies. Joey, they’re my favorite. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And, a brownie! Well, half a brownie. Actually, it’s just bag. It’s been a long walk from the flower shop and I was startin’ to feel faint so…
lines.stage_direction: Hands her a bag with the brownie in it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh man! This is so great! I actually feel like I’m going on a real date! Although, I have a hint of morning sickness, and I’m wearing underwear that goes up to about……there.
lines.stage_direction: She snaps the waistband on her underwear that is just slightly below her breasts

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey come on now, this is a real date. Uh, so…nice place you got here. Foosball, huh? Pizza box. Oh, a subscription to Playboy , my kind of woman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, actually that’s my roommate’s.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I would like to meet him. He sounds like a stand up guy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah yes, but he’s very protective of me so you’d better watch yourself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah… Hey, so this roommate of yours…is he good looking?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hm-mmm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, it must be tough to keep your hands of him, huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but I’m pretty sure he’s gay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no-no, he’s not! No! Why are you trying to ruin the game? Come on!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is getting ready for her date with Joey as there is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: …which brings us back, of course, to Greely’s theory of dominance. Okay, that-that’s all for today. Oh, uh does anyone know where the Freeman building is?

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Yeah, it’s the new building on Avenue A.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! That-that’s all the way cross town, I’m supposed to teach a graduate seminar there in ten minutes.

lines.speaker: Student
lines.text: Ooh, dude. That’s not gonna happen.

scenes.scene_description: N.Y.U, Ross is teaching a class.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Move it! Move it! Move it!! Hey!!! I’m the teacher!!

scenes.scene_description: Ross grabs his things and runs to the door only to be blocked by his exiting students.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello. I’m sorry I’m a little late. Ah——Whoa! A lot late. Let me start by uh, by introducing myself, I am Professor Geller. So to sum up, I’m Professor Geller. Good job today.
lines.stage_direction: He throws his jacket towards the coat rack and misses. Checks his watch The bell rings.

scenes.scene_description: The Freeman Building, Ross is entering his new class completely out of breath.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now the filet mignon, what comes with that?

lines.speaker: Waitress
lines.text: There’s a side of steamed vegetables.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Emmm. Now, instead of the vegetables, is there anyway I can substitute the three-pound lobster?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y’know what? Bring her both, and I’ll have the same.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! This is shaping up to be a pretty good date—Oh, I almost forgot. I didn’t pay you the rent check.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh whoa-whoa-whoa, no roommate stuff. Okay? We’re on a date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Wow! So I get to see what Joey Tribbiani is like on a date. So do you have any moves?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No. Umm, just myself and if they don’t like me for— I’m sorry I couldn’t even get through that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I knew it! I knew it. Come on tell me your moves.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh alright. Umm, well, okay, I usually start by having a bottle of wine sent to my table from a fan.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. And that works?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well it does when you combine it with, "This is so embarrassing, I just want to have a normal life!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you poor little famous man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh okay, how about this one. I was gonna wait until the end of the night to kiss you, but you’re so beautiful…I don’t think I can.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Wow! That was fantastic, I almost leaned in. I really almost did!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, so…so tell me one of your moves.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright. So where’d you grow up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s your move? Boy Rach, you’re lucky you’re hot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, just answer the question!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Queens.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And so were-were you close to your parents?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, with my mom. Yeah, not so much with my dad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know. I guess there’s just always been this distance y’know—I mean we both try to pretend it’s not there, but it is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. It’s gotta be rough.
lines.stage_direction: sympathetic Starts rubbing his wrist with her fingers.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it is. It’s really tough. Y’know sometimes I think—Wow!! Nice move!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: "Where’d you grow up," it’s so simple!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! And now if you’ll excuse me, I have to go to the rest room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

scenes.scene_description: A Restaurant, a waitress is taking Joey and Rachel’s dinner order.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And now you’re watching me walk away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes I am! Again so simple!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel gets up and heads for the rest room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You are not going to believe what I did today!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well it clearly wasn’t showering or shaving.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got good. I played this game all day and now I rule at it! They should change the name of it to Ms. Chandler. Although I-I hope they don’t.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, you stayed home all day and played Ms. Pac-Man while I went off to work like some kind of chump?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-huh, and I got all the top ten scores, I erased Phoebe off the board! High five!
lines.stage_direction: Holds up his hand to give Monica a high five, only he can’t straighten his fingers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is the matter with your hand?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I’ve been playing it for like eight hours, it’ll loosen up. Come on, check out the scores. Oh, and also look at the initials, they’re dirty words.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, why would you do that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because it’s awesome.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You think this is clever?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well y’know, they only give you three letters, so after A-S-S it is a bit of a challenge.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey wait a minute, this one isn’t dirty.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It is when you put it together with that one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah, well if you don’t clear this off, you won’t be getting one of those from me. But Ben’s coming over here tomorrow to play this game, this can’t be there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on, he won’t even know what they mean.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler! He’s seven; he’s not stupid.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Have you talked to him lately?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, look I’m just gonna unplug it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no, if you unplug it, I’ll have nothing to show for my day! It would be like I was at work. No! Hey look at that! Look at that, it’s still there! This thing must have some kind of primitive ROM Chip in it or something!
lines.stage_direction: She unplugs it. And plugs it back in. Read Only Memory, it’s a memory chip.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey you-you got to beat your scores!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: With the claw?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right fine. Fine, I’ll do it. I’ve just got to get this off the screen. Carol and Susan are still upset that you taught him pull my finger.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pull my finger——My hand is messed up.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is entering to find Chandler playing Ms. Pac-Man .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am not gonna answer that!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh come on! Just pick one! Between Monica, Phoebe, Chandler, and Ross if you had to, if you had to, who would you punch?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No one! They are my friends, I wouldn’t punch any of them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but I don’t know why. Look at me, I’m having such a wonderful time!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too! Hey Rach, can I just say I think this is the best date I ever had!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I never laughed so hard—Did you see the wine come out of my nose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, I think everyone saw the wine come out of your nose.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I gotta say, I never knew I could enjoy the non-sex part of the date so much.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that is because you have never been on a date with me before.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh. Huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, now don’t judge me. I normally wait until my date leaves, but you live here. I’m ripping into this swan.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah! Okay, well then you don’t judge me. I’m gonna suck on the cellophane from the brownie I had before.
lines.stage_direction: They both do as they planned.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So tell me, what are Joey Tribbiani’s end of the night moves?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, well, if I want the girl to kiss me, first thing I do is make my lips look irresistible.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How do you do that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now you can’t tell anyone, but uh…I put on shiny lip balm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, like a moth to a flame, I’m telling ya. Okay all right, so now you go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I don’t want to tell you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because it’s embarrassing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: More embarrassing than shiny raspberry lip balm?! I didn’t say raspberry before did I? All right just-just tell me Rach, just tell me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. All right, stand up. Well, when we’re at the door, I lightly press my lips against his, and then move into his body just for a second, and then I make this sound, "Hmmm." Okay, I know it doesn’t sound like anything, but I swear it works.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Why—Yeah, that would work for ya…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I gotta go to bed. Honey, I had such a wonderful time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Yeah. Me to.
lines.stage_direction: She kisses him. She goes into her room. He then starts to freak out.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Rachel are returning from their date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: …you were 50 minutes late to the class, what did you crawl there?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I ran. It was really far, and when did people stop understanding the phrase, "Get the hell out of my way!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, why didn’t you just take a cab?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh, between the traffic that time of day and all the one-way streets it’ll take me twice as long. Besides, I teach the class three times a week, who am I? Rockefeller?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well you’re not gonna be able to keep doing this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I have to. Okay? If I don’t, they’ll take the class away from me. And…I already put it in my family newsletter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’ve seen it, the Geller Yeller.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right. Wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Besides, I-I think I figured out a much faster route, I’m sure I can make it this time. I just…I just can’t be afraid to get a little bit…hit by cars.
lines.stage_direction: He goes to the bathroom as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Hey, remember how last night we were talking about that movie Cujo ?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, I still can’t believe you haven’t seen Cujo . What is wrong with you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Relax! It’s not like it’s Citizen Kane !

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Have you ever tried to sit through Citizen Kane ?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I know it’s really boring, but it’s like a big deal. Anyway, I was thinking about renting Cujo sometime.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah? All right, let’s do it tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well don’t you have that big date tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey, can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: After our date last night, did you feel a little weird?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! You did too? It totally freaked me out, what was that?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know! I’m-I’m kinda thinking it-it was the lobster…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah-yeah, the lobster.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I mean I was up sick all night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah me too, all night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?! How come we didn’t cross paths?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah well that’s because uh…I stayed in my room. Yeah, you don’t want to look in my hamper.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Ross is telling Rachel about his class location predicament.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I got that. I’ll escape over there. I’ll come back over here. All right, come on Ms. Pac-Man . It’s got—Right— Well, you’re just a little bitch, aren’t you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, you guys. Listen, I’m sorry that I was hogging the game before——Oh my God! Your friends have some unfortunate initials!
lines.stage_direction: entering Sees the top ten list

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They’re all Chandler.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler sucks! He couldn’t have gotten this good!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I did. But it came at a price.
lines.stage_direction: Looks at his still deformed hand

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But Ben is coming over tonight and he can’t see this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on, by age seven kids have already seen orgies. Was it just me?!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is trying to erase Chandler’s dirty words while he looks on.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!!! Yes, I made it! I’m on time! Okay, why don’t we all uh, open our books to page 23. Where… Where you will see…a uh…a bunch of uh…red spots. Okay, umm, why don’t, why don’t you all start to read, while I—
lines.stage_direction: Grabs a bottle of water from a student, takes a drink, and splashes some on his face like the marathon runners do. Exhales loudly Exhales again Closes his book. Passes out and collapses.

scenes.scene_description: N.Y.U, Ross’s new class, he is entering out of breath.

lines.speaker: Joey's Date
lines.text: Hey. Are you all right? You seem a little distracted.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no! I’m fine. It’s just… Hey, can I ask you something? Have you ever looked at someone that you’ve known for a while and then suddenly…suddenly see them a different way?

lines.speaker: Joey's Date
lines.text: You mean like from behind?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, that…that’s exactly it. You’re right. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey's Date
lines.text: Ew, y’know what? One time I saw this guy from behind and he seemed like a totally normal guy and then he turned around and it was Stephan Baldwin!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh! Yeah. So yeah, so you know exactly what I’m talking about.

lines.speaker: Joey's Date
lines.text: Totally! Wow! Would you excuse me for a sec?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, sure. Ehhh.
lines.stage_direction: She gets up, walks towards the bathroom, and Joey watches her go.

scenes.scene_description: A Restaurant, Joey is on his hot date and they’re not speaking right now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God Phoebe, you’re on fire!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: One more score to go! You can do it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don’t touch me!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don’t touch her!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! Go left! Go left! Go right!! Go right!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t!! I can’t!! Noooooooo!!!!!!! You son of a bitch!!!!!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, now it’s Phoebe’s turn to erase Chandler from the board.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hi Ben—No!! Don’t look at the machine!

scenes.scene_description: At this point a stream of obscenities burst forth from Phoebe’s mouth just in time for Ben and Ross to enter and hear most of it, and in slow motion Ross tries to shield his innocent son from Phoebe’s vulgarity.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God! Thank God you’re home! I’m watching Cujo .

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alone?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! But what is wrong with this dog?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, did you get to the part where they’re trapped in the car and Cujo’s throwin’ himself at the windshield?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No! Seriously, what’s wrong with the dog?! Wait a minute, what are you doing home so early? What happened to your date?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, it didn’t work out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Do you want to watch the rest of the movie with me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, okay. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know, I never thought I’d say this about a movie, but I really hope this dog dies. What are you doing over there? Come sit here, you protect me.
lines.stage_direction: Joey brings over a stool at sits on it next to Rachel who’s in the big chair.

lines.speaker: Joey

lines.text: But Joey, I don’t think Ross wants me to move into his apartment and disrupt his life like that. I mean——Or he does.
lines.stage_direction: Ross turns to her with wishful eyes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No I-I-I would love to be around for you and the baby. And we-we can just try it like on a temporary basis.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But Ross, its you and me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So? Sure! But it-it wouldn’t be anything romantic. And I’m-I’m dating Mona—Damnit Mona! I was supposed to meet her like an hour ago! What is wrong with me?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right now, so? What do you think?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know. Is it crazy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No it’s not. Joey, this is a smart idea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I was due.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, let’s do it. I’ll move in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh Rach that’s great. That’s great.
lines.stage_direction: They hug and Joey breathes a sigh of relief.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is balancing an aluminum can on her stomach as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not to shabby, I got this all off myself…using my wife’s tools. Oh my God!
lines.stage_direction: He takes the door off the frame and we finally get to see what’s behind the green door! It is stacked, floor to ceiling, with junk.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How did you get in there?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’re messy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no! You weren’t supposed to see this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I married Fred Sanford!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No Chandler, you don’t understand! Okay! Okay! Okay! Fine! Now you know. Okay? I’m y’know…I’m sick.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler starts singing the theme for Sanford and Son , an old TV show starring Redd Fox.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, honey you’re not sick! Look, I don’t love you because you’re organized, I love you in spite of that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really? You promise you won’t tell anyone?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! And look, now that I know if I got some extra stuff lying around can we, can we share the closet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well…it’s just umm…I’m afraid you might mess it up.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is pounding out the hinge pins on the closet door to get it open.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So Rachel’s all moved out huh? How are you taking it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well uh, I wanted to have a few beers, but uh, I got rid of those because Rachel couldn’t stand the smell of them. But I have thrown back a lot of orange juice with calcium though. And uh, it’s a couple weeks past it’s expiration date, so it’s got a bit of a kick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kidding me? I’m great! Yeah, I’m uh; I’m better than great. I am good. And now that she’s gone, I can uh, I can do all this stuff around here that I couldn’t do before. Y’know? Like umm, I can walk around naked again. Y’know? I can uh, I can watch porn in the living room. Right? This is uh, this is good for me. Y’know? I like being on my own, I’m uh, better off this way. I’m uh, a lone wolf. Y’know? A loner. Alone. All alone. Forever. What’s a wolf got to do to get a huh around here?!
lines.stage_direction: Chandler rushes over and hugs him.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey is drinking a gallon of orange juice as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohhh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s it. Just relax.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh. Oh! Ohh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on more!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you like that don’t you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah right there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You want it there? You take it there baby!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, say my name. Say it! And now I’m going to cover you back up, and umm we’re never doing this again.
lines.stage_direction: She stops when she realizes what she’s doing.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's Apartment, Phoebe is giving Monica another massage.

title: The One Where Joey Tells Rachel

lines.speaker: Lisa Kudrow
lines.text: Previously on Friends

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you um—I’m sorry, did you just say it’s Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um, you…you like Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes. I like Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, okay but look, buy uh—Hey-hey, y’know, y’know who else I like? You! And it-it doesn’t get said enough. I like you Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But R-R-Rachel-Rachel?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but it’s not a big deal.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s not a big deal? Oh, I’m sorry I just…um, I…what about all the stuff you-you just said? I mean how about, I like—you-you can’t stop thinking about her. Like how you can’t sleep?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m an actor, y’know? As-as a group, we tend to be over dramatic.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel who’s carrying my baby? Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look no, I-I know it’s bad, and I know it’s wrong. Okay? But-but it’s not like anything’s ever gonna happen. Y’know? These-these are just feelings, they’re gonna go away.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know what? I-I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh come on Ross! Hey Ross-Ross don’t…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just—y’know—I-I just have one—Rachel?!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey is talking to Ross

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Rachel?!!

scenes.scene_description: He exits and starts to walk away, passes a window, stops, and says "Rachel?!" again. Joey sighs and turns around to face Gunther.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh! Okay, she’s kicking!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! She’s growing inside you.
lines.stage_direction: Puts his hand on her belly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow that was a big one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think that’s the youngest girl ever to reject me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hey you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey listen let me ask you, do you believe in soul mates?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yes I do. I do. I believe that there is one perfect person out there for everyone. And do you know how you find him? You stop looking for him. That’s why I stopped looking for Russell Crowe. He’ll find me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh, and you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. I mean I believe that uh, certain people are more suited for each other and I believe in falling in love, but soul mates, I don’t think they exist.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well last night, I met Monica’s.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I had a date with this guy, and I swear to God, he is her other half.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on, don’t be crazy. You don’t think there’s someone out there better suited for Monica than me, do ya?
lines.stage_direction: To Rachel Rachel looks at him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what’s he like?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well he’s tall.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He has brown hair.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course, of course.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A tall guy with hair similar to mine, oh unknowable universe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He works with food!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh sure. Older?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Obviously, and he’s British.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I was just gonna ask!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah? And, he’s-he’s so centered and mature and confident.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it’s so sad they never had a chance to meet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Luckily, the guy she settled for can’t hear what you’re talking about.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I’m so sorry. Um, and maybe I’m wrong! I but—y’know I’m gonna go out with him again, I’ll find out more.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You believe that this guy is destined for someone else and you’re still gonna date him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he may not be my soul mate, but a girl’s gotta eat.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Rachel and Chandler are on the couch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just talked to Joey, I just—I want to see how you were.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh y’know, I still—I can’t believe it. Joey and Rachel I mean it’s… It’s like you and me going out, only weirder!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I know you’re hurting, and-and I want to be supportive, but don’t say that again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! What if, what if they get married? Then he’d be the stepfather of my child.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, I don’t think that’s something we need to worry about! First of all he’s-he’s never gonna tell her how he feels about her. And even if he did you have no idea how she’d react.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, because women never like Joey. Y’know, I think he’s a virgin.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey…he’s not even thinking about going after Rachel!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He’s not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! All he’s thinking about is how you’re taking this! I mean, listen it’s totally freaking him out. He’s talking about moving to Vermont.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He says he wants to leave the country. He thinks you hate him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hate him? I… No, I don’t hate him. It’s just it’s Rachel, y’know?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey I can’t even imagine how hard this must be for you. But, I don’t want you to lose Joey over it. And right now he just needs to know that you’re still his friend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay, I’ll talk to him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. Now do it soon, he just asked me how to convert his dollars into Vermont money.

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Rachel's, Ross is opening the door to Monica.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, Chandler, Monica, this is Don.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Oh, hello. Hello.
lines.stage_direction: Shakes Chandler’s hand. Shakes Monica’s hand.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Soul mate.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Soul mate.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, what have you guys been doing?

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Well, we just had a terrible lunch today at Reattica. What is with all the sun-dried tomatoes at that place?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! What is this, 1985?!

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: That’s exactly what I said. Phoebe, isn’t that strange.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not really.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What’s wrong with sun-dried tomatoes? On a barbecue chicken pizza? No?
lines.stage_direction: Everyone stares at him. Still there’s staring.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So Don, what-what other restaurants do you like?

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Well, Octavio, 27&7—Oh! And there’s this great little place, Alessandro’s.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! That’s my restaurant, I’m the chef there.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: You’re kidding me!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Your food is fantastic! Wow, I really want to talk to you about your menu, once I get some coffees first. Um, anyone want any?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I’d like a latte. Oh y’know what? If you’re gonna talk about me, I’m gonna go with you.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Monica and Chandler are on the couch as Phoebe enters with Monica’s soul mate.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nothing! I swear to God I didn’t know you guys would be here! And the good news is you didn’t believe in soul mates. So…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I believe in tall, handsome strangers who hit on my wife!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They’re just talking, and y’know what? Just because I think they’re soul mates doesn’t mean anything’s gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, good work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Oh he’s great, I love him.
lines.stage_direction: Walks away and Chandler glares at Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don’t worry, we’ll find you someone else.

scenes.scene_description: They go over to the counter and Chandler moves closer to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I understand if you came by to hit me, I deserve it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I don’t want to hit you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh what then? Kick me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bite me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no I don’t want to do anything to you. All right? I just want to tell you that I’m not mad at you and…and that I certainly do not hate you. I just, I just came here to say that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh now—Hey Ross-Ross! Do you wanna-wanna come in for a beer or something?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh…yeah sure.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do uh, do you got any beer? All-all I got is this melon stuff that Rachel left. I don’t…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey look Ross, you need to understand something okay? I uh…I am never gonna act on this Rachel thing, okay? I-I would never do anything to jeopardize my friendship with you.
lines.stage_direction: pouring two glasses He hands Ross a glass.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

scenes.scene_description: Joey’s Apartment, there’s a knock on the door and Joey answers it to Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Anyway, it uh…look it’ll just…take me a while to get over her, that’s all. I’m not even sure how to do that, I mean I’ve never been in love before so…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! You’re in love with her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I thought you knew that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow. Hey look, if it helps, I don’t want to feel this way. Honest. I just keep thinking, "Ah, I’ll get over this." Y’know? I just—It just keeps gettin’ harder. I don’t, I don’t know what to do. Y’know? What do I do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think you need to tell her how you feel.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay that’s the green stuff talkin’.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I’m serious. You-you need to find out where she is, because if she’s not where you are, then you can start to move past this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But what if uh—and I’m not saying she will be—But…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: If she is where you are then uh…then my feeling weird about it shouldn’t stand in the way.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Look if-if she’s gonna end up with somebody else, the truth is she couldn’t find a better guy. So…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So when do you think you’re gonna talk to her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God I have to tell her! I haven’t even thought about what I will say. What should I say?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m understanding, but let’s not get carried away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m sorry, you’re right. What am I gonna say? Oh!
lines.stage_direction: He takes another sip of the green stuff and recoils at the taste.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, tell me about it.
lines.stage_direction: He stands up, turns his back to Joey, and enjoys another sip.

scenes.scene_description: They clink glasses and take a drink. Ross likes it, Joey hates it. Then Ross sees Joey hating it, mimics he hates it as well.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: …so I’ve been slowly phasing out the wine importing and focusing more on the cheese side of things.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Cheese you say? That’s some pretty smelly work, huh Don?

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Cheese, it’s smelly. You must smell a lot of the time too.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Uh, not really. But when it comes to cheese, I’m one of the people who thinks the smellier the better.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me too! Yeah, Chandler can’t stand it. He won’t even allow me to have blue cheese in the house.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: And you’re still married to him?
lines.stage_direction: They both laugh, and Chandler tries to but fails.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know what I’d love to do? I would like to go to France and eat nothing but bread and cheese—Not even bread, just cheese. No, I want the bread. Yeah. Ah, and pastries… And pate. Oh, I’m really not high, it’s just I used to be fat.

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: Well if you where ever enter the Loire valley let me know, I’ve got a great little villa you can stay at.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is it made of cheese?

lines.speaker: Don
lines.text: No. But God, a house made of cheese, wouldn’t that be incredible?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’d move in tomorrow!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on! Are you listening to this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m so sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What do we do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t know, they both want to live in a house of cheese! I don’t know how you fight that.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe and Chandler are watching Monica and Don talk.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Okay, I can do this. I can tell her how I feel. Just uh, just stand up straight. Take a couple deep breaths. Look confident.
lines.stage_direction: Does so. Does so. Does so as Rachel opens the door and startles him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey? What’s up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I uh, I just came by because I-I want to talk to you about something.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay what’s up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here? In the hall? What are we animals?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well honey, I’m late for a meeting. So can you just make it quick?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay umm, I just came by to tell you that I…want to have dinner with you tonight. That’s all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure! That sounds great! Just leave me a message and tell me where to meet you. Okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. That’s good. Okay, that give you a couple hours to prepare what you’re gonna say. Good. Yeah. Don’t you people ever knock?!
lines.stage_direction: Someone comes out of his apartment and it startles Joey again.

scenes.scene_description: Outside Ross and Rachel's, Joey walks up to knock on the door, but stops.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’d like to have Don and Phoebe over. Wouldn’t that be nice?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure, why don’t you set it up. I’ll just be over here, browsing through the personals.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you okay? You’ve been acting weird all afternoon.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, fine. Fine. Not perfect!! But good enough.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Jeez! What is with you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m sorry, did you say cheese?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, what’s going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe thinks you and Don are soul mates, and I don’t believe in that kind of stuff. But then you two totally get along. So look, I won’t stand in your way if you want to run off with Don and live in a house of cheese.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, you don’t believe in soul mates?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. But I’m sure ‘tomatoes’ does.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t believe in soul mates either.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don’t?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. I don’t think that you and I were destined to end up together. I think that we fell in love and work hard at our relationship. Some days we work really hard.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you…you don’t want to live with Don in a cheese house?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I’ve had second thoughts about that. Do you realize how hard that would be to clean?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know what? I am going to take you out to dinner tonight. I found this place that makes the greatest mozzarella sticks and jalepino poppers . No? Really? They taste so good.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are entering.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: …and I know Chandler is kidding but it happens every time he touches my stomach. I mean I’m really worried the baby’s not going to like him. Are you okay?
lines.stage_direction: Joey is staring at the table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? Yeah! Sure! Uh, look at the uh, the reason…—Is it hot in here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Not-not for me, but why don’t you take off your sweater?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I would, but this is a nice place and my T-shirt has a picture of Calvin doing Hobbs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Really?! Can I see it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Sure.
lines.stage_direction: They both half stand up, Joey pulls the neck of his sweater out, and Rachel looks down it to see his T-shirt.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. Wow, I wouldn’t think Hobbs would like that so much.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh… How long have we known each other?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, seven…e-e-eight, eight years. Wow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-huh, long time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But over the past few weeks…

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Hah, sorry about the wait, but it is mega-jammed in here! We have a couple specials tonight…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually uh, could you give us a second?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Sure. Sure. Second’s up! Not…that kind of table.
lines.stage_direction: Turns away, then turns back Joey glares at him. He walks away.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So you were saying?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m not quite sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well you had asked me how long we had known each other, and I said, "Eight years." And the um, waiter came over and cut his tip in half, and umm…now here we are.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, here we are. Uhh… I… I think I’m…falling in love with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m falling in love with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who are you talking too? Oh, you’re kidding! Oh, it’s a joke! It’s funny. It’s funny. I don’t get it. Oh. Okay. Umm… I-I…uh, wow. Are you uh… How did umm… When?
lines.stage_direction: looking around Laughs. Joey doesn’t say any thing and Rachel realizes it’s not a joke. Pause

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Does it really matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! Wow. Wow. Wow, it is hot in here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay look Rach, I know this is a lot. You don’t have to say anything. You-you uh, you take as much time as you need. Okay, you gotta say something!
lines.stage_direction: Long pause as Rachel says nothing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, Joey I love you so much, but I…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah right. That’s okay. That’s fine. That’s uh, pretty much what I was expecting. So uh, it’s no big deal. All right? I think I’m gonna go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Joey please! Please don’t! Please don’t leave like this! Now come on, you cannot do this to a pregnant woman!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don’t start doing that. You can’t do that Rach, ‘cause then you’re gonna make me do that. Oh, here we go!
lines.stage_direction: Starts to cry. Sits down next to her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can I?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure!

scenes.scene_description: A waiter runs over interrupting Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Joey honey I don’t…I don’t want to lose…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey, hey! You can’t. Okay? Ever!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m so sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no-no Rach, please, don’t be sorry. Okay? Don’t be sorry. Y’know I was only kidding you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, that was a real good one.

lines.text: Is this your table?

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.text: What’s the matter with them?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: I don’t know. I think maybe one of them is dying. I kinda hope it’s the girl. The guy is really cute!

title: The One With The Tea Leaves

lines.text: Sure.

lines.text: I can’t! Y’know? You guys don’t know what it’s like to put yourself out there like that and just get shot down.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right so, so what do I do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is Rachel. I mean, what are you gonna do, never going to talk to her again? I mean I know it’s weird, it’s awkward, but you gotta at least try.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mona? Okay, if I were a salmon shirt, where would I be?
lines.stage_direction: There’s no answer, so he starts to leave but remembers where her extra key is. He reaches atop a hall light just outside her door and grabs the key. He looks at in triumph as the pain from it being hot moves along his nervous system to his brain, and when it arrives his brain orders his hand to drop the hot key and his mouth to squeal in pain. After dropping the key he pulls his shirtsleeve over his hand and uses the key to open the door and enter Mona’s apartment. He hears a key in the door and as it opens he dives behind the couch.
