lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh my God! Ross!!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello!!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Ross, what are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not touching myself if that makes anyone less uncomfortable.

scenes.scene_description: Mona’s Apartment, she and her date are making out as Ross flips through a magazine while lying behind the couch and sees something that he likes. Meanwhile, Mona’s date takes off Ross’s shirt and Mona throws it on the floor. While they start making out again, Ross tries to pull the rug the shirt is on over to him, but while he does that he moves the coffee table and it bumps into the couch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Morning. You wanted to see me?

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Please, come in. Have a seat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay look Mr. Zelner…

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Oh I think it’s best that I speak first.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: I’ve asked Lee from human resources to be here as a witness to our conversation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: If I in any way implied that I wanted to buy your baby…I am sorry. Okay? Last week when I asked you when your due date was uh, I certainly did not mean that I felt that I was due your baby. Yeah, I want to be very clear that I understand that its your baby, and it is not mine to purchase.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, as long as we are clear about that.

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Zelner’s Office, Rachel is knocking on the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh there it is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s not your regular dry cleaners.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, but that creep that I went on that date with goes to there so I have to find a new one. I also have to find a new video store, a new bank, a new adult bookstore, a new grocery store…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A new grocery store. The universe said I was going to meet a nice guy and that’s what they gave me? When I get up there I’m going to kick some ass.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don’t worry Phoebe, you’re gonna meet someone. If I can meet a great guy, so can you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, we both can. And we both will.

scenes.scene_description: A Street, Phoebe and Monica are walking down it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you didn’t have to come in with me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you kidding? This is where they get out stains! Okay? This is like Disneyland for me. I’m-I’m gonna be over here watching the dance of the clean shirts.
lines.stage_direction: She points to and walks over to the electric clothes rack they have.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: They enter the dry cleaners.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Oh, excuse me! I think you dropped s… Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: I’m sorry, it’s just that you’re so incredibly beautiful.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah well, I’m sorry about that too, but what are you going to do?

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: I hope you don’t think I’m crazy but I feel like I was meant to pick this up, do you believe in that kind of thing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A little. Now you’re talking.
lines.stage_direction: She turns around and looks up. To the universe

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Would you like to go out and have a cup of coffee?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I-I’d love to. Let me just tell my friend.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ooh, an ink stain! Hey, can I watch how you get this out?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She must’ve left.

scenes.scene_description: A guy enters that looks suspiciously like Alec Baldwin from The Hunt for Red October , Pearl Harbor , and Beetlejuice .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So? What-what-what happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It’s all gonna be okay. They’re just so happy that I’m not suing them that they gave me one extra month paid maternity leave. So long as I understand that the money should not construed as a down payment on this or any other child I should bear.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, Ralph Lauren is really going out of there way to show they’re not in the baby buying business.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, can you give us a minute?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh I’m sorry, you’re kicking me out of my own living room?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’ll be in there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, I’m really sorry that I lied to you. I was just trying to make things…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know. I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It kinda worked. I mean y’know, I don’t know about you buy I haven’t thought about our thing since all this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey you’re right. Yeah, it’s kinda been like us again a little bit.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I know! I miss that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too. I mean I…haven’t thought at all about how I put myself out there and said all that stuff and how you didn’t feel the same way about me and-and how it was really awkward.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is watching Joey pace nervously as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My gynecologist tried to kill me.

scenes.scene_description: Awkward silence.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Item J437-A, color: winterberry. Hi umm, listen come here, come in. I’m so-so sorry about yesterday. I-I’m really sorry. It’s just that I…
lines.stage_direction: on phone There’s a knock on the door as he hangs up the phone. He answers it to Mona. She does so. He picks up the pink shirt.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Listen Ross, you don’t have to apologize. I understand why you were there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yeah, you still have feelings for me. And-and to be honest, I-I still have feelings for you. And I wish that we can work it out Ross, but we can’t. It’s too complicated with you and Rachel and the baby, I-I just… It just wasn’t meant to be.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh God you’re right.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Ross, we…we have to be strong. Okay, I-I’m gonna go. Can I? To remember you?
lines.stage_direction: She picks up the shirt Ross has just set down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Rachel's, Ross is ordering something over the phone from a catalog.

title: The One In Massapequa

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Ross, Mon, is it okay if I bring someone to your parent’s anniversary party?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, who’s the guy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, his name is Parker and I met him at the drycleaners.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oooh, did he put a little starch in your bloomers? Who said that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, he’s really great though. He has this incredible zest for life, and he treats me like a queen, except at night when he treats me like the naughty girl I am.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, by the way. Would it be okay if I gave the toast to mom and dad this year?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, yeah, you sure you want to after what happened at their 20 th ?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I’d really like to.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, hopefully this time mom won’t boo you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Every year Ross makes the toast, and it’s always really moving, and always makes them cry. Well this year I’m going to make them cry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And you wonder why Ross is their favorite?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Really! Any time Ross makes a toast everyone cries, and hugs him, and pats him on the back and they all come up to me and say, "God, your brother." Know what they’ll say this year? "God, you"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I can promise you, at least one person will be crying. I’m an actor, and any actor worth himself can cry on cue.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really you can do that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kidding me? Watch! Well I can’t do it with you guys watching me!
lines.stage_direction: Makes funny faces trying to cry

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, everyone is there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh I’m working on my toast for the party, or as I like to call it. Sob fest 2002. Hey check this out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It’s a dog.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s a dead dog. That’s Chi-Chi; she died when I was in high school.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It’s your parents’ anniversary and you’re going to talk about their dead pet?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The good stuff, huh?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica’s, they’re getting ready to leave for the party.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You got a present for my parents. That’s so sweet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, in honor of their 35 th wedding anniversary, I had a star named after them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aww that is so cool.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I got them a book on Karma Sutra for the elderly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, do you guys have any extra ribbon?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, sure. What do you need? We got lace, satin, sateen, raffia, gingham, felt, and I think my testacles may be in here too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aww! Chi-Chi! Oh, I loved this dog! Y’know Monica couldn’t get braces because Chi-Chi needed knee surgery.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You were the 200-pound 11-year-old who rode her!

scenes.scene_description: Ross, Joey, and Rachel enter

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Everybody, this is Parker, Parker this is…

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: No, no, no wait! Don’t tell me. Let me guess. Joey, Monica, Ross, Rachel and, I’m sorry Phoebe didn’t mention you. Chandler, I’m kidding all ready you’re my favorite!
lines.stage_direction: Points as he says their names Chandler makes a face

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ha!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Why don’t all of you tell me a little about your self?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, actually, I’m sorry we-we probably should get going.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Classic Ross. Rachel, Rachel, oh how you glow. May I?
lines.stage_direction: laughs Puts hand on her stomach

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I, uh, think you already are.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Rachel, you have life growing inside you. Is there anything in this world more miraculous than—Oh a picture of a dog! Whose is this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s my old dog. He passed away years ago.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Oh well, at least you were lucky to have him. Bow-wow old friend, bow-wow. So where’s the party?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s out on the island. It’s in Massapequa.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Maaaassapequa, sounds like a magical place. Tell me about Massapequa, is it steep in Native American history? {Transcriber’s Note: Interestingly Alec Baldwin was born in Massapequa.}

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, there is an Arby’s in the shape of a tee-pee.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I got my note cards. Do you got the presents?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And I’ve got the car keys.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: We’re driving!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Aces!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe and Parker enter

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So uh, he seems like a nice guy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah I like him a lot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ya wanna hang back and take our own cab?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, otherwise I’m not going.

scenes.scene_description: Everyone except Ross and Rachel leave.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! Hey mom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This is such a great party! 35 years. Very impressive, do you guys have any pearls of wisdom?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Jack?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Why would you serve food on such a sharp stick?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s a good question, dad. That’s a good question…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmmm….

lines.speaker: an
lines.text: Congratulations you two!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you…we’re so excited

lines.speaker: an
lines.text: And also, congratulations on your wedding.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha—What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Can we talk to you for just a y’know… It’s just a little thing. Well we think it’s absolutely marvelous that you’re having this baby out of wedlock, some of our friends are less open-minded. Which is why we’ve told them all that you’re married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Thanks for going along with this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad so what we have to pretend that we’re married?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Son, I had to shave my ears for tonight. You can do this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can you believe that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, if you’re going to do the ears, you might as well take a pass at the nosal area.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, us having to lie about being married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I know I don’t either, but ya know what, it’s their party, and it’s just one night. And we don’t even have to lie; we just won’t say anything. If it comes up again, we’ll just…smile. We’ll nod along.

lines.speaker: an
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Rachel!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Aunt Lisa, Uncle Dan

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: Congratulations on the baby, and on the wedding

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmmmm….

lines.speaker: Uncle Dan
lines.text: Here’s a little something to get you started.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh…

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: So, how’s married life treating you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Unbelievable!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We love marriage!

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: Great!

scenes.scene_description: The Anniversary Party, Ross and Rachel are arriving and see his parents.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: What a beautiful place. What a great night! I have to tell you, being here with all of you in Event Room C…I feel so lucky. I think of all the good times that have happened here. The birthdays, the proms, the mitzvahs both bar and bat, but none of them will compare with tonight! My God, I don’t want to forget this moment! It’s like I want to take a mental picture of you all! Click!
lines.stage_direction: He takes a mental picture of them all.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t think the flash went off.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Dahaaa! I’m going to find the men’s room, be right back.
lines.stage_direction: Punches Chandler in the arm and he makes a face of pain.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’ll go with you

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Come on!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Somewhere there is someone with a tranquilizer gun and a huge butterfly net looking for that man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I have to go to the bathroom too, but I don’t want him complimenting my thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m so we weren’t in the car! Did he ever let up?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He called the Long Island Expressway a concrete miracle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This room! This night! That waiter! His shoes! I must take a mental picture! Ooh sorry…
lines.stage_direction: imitating Parker He backs into someone. He looks behind him then notices its Phoebe then stops his impression.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Were you guys making fun of Parker?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That depends, how much did you hear?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, he a little enthusiastic, what’s wrong with that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s just that, it’s so much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, so what I like him! Do I make fun of the people you’ve dated? Tag, Janice, Mona? No, because friends don’t do that. But, do you want my opinion? Do you want it? ‘Cause in my opinion, your collective dating record reads like the who’s who of human crap.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I feel terrible.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What was wrong with Mona?

scenes.scene_description: The rest of the gang arrives including Parker.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Open it! Open it! Open it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah baby!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: So we never got to hear about your wedding!

lines.speaker: an
lines.text: We were surprise that we weren’t invited.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, it was just our parents and 1 or 2 friends. It was a small wedding.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But it was beautiful. I mean it was small, but kind of spectacular.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Where did you have it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: On a cliff, in Barbados, at sunset, and Stevie Wonder sang Isn’t She Lovely as I walked down the aisle.

lines.speaker: an
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, Stevie’s an old family friend.

lines.speaker: an
lines.text: Oh my God. That sounds amazing. I would love to see pictures.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So would I. You wouldn’t think that Annie Liebawitz would forget to put film in the camera.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Would you excuse us for a second? Umm…. what are you doing?
lines.stage_direction: Pulls Rachel off to the side

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? I’m not you. This may be the only wedding I ever have. I want it to be amazing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay. Ooooh, ooh maybe I rode in on a Harley .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, Ross, it has to be realistic.

scenes.scene_description: The Anniversary Party, Ross and Rachel have just gotten another wedding present.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Are you okay? You seem kind of quiet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I’m fine. I’m great. I’m with you.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: And I’m with you! What a great time to be alive! Look at this plate-bouncy thing. What an inspired solution to man’s plate dispensing problems.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hm huh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Ah! Oysters! Let me feed you one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, that’s not necessary.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Please.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, actually I don’t eat…

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I won’t quit until you try.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, fine! Fine! Mmm…hmmmmm….
lines.stage_direction: Takes the oyster and pretends to eat it while dropping it on the floor

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: What are they like? I’ve never had one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why don’t you just try one?

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: No, they look too weird.

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Phoebe and Parker

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doin’?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just going over my toast. Those two will never know what hit ‘em. I can’t wait. They’re going to be crying so hard. They’re going to be fighting for breath.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ya know if you want to, I can just hold them down and you could .

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Monica and Chandler

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And my veil was lace, made by blind, Belgium nuns.

lines.speaker: an
lines.text: Blind?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, not at first, but it was very intricate work and they said even though they lost their sight, it was all worth it.

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: I’ll bet you looked beautiful…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I don’t know about that, but some said that I looked like a floating angel.

lines.speaker: an
lines.text: So, how did you propose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. That’s a great story.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, um, actually, I-I took her to the planetarium. That’s-that’s where we had our first date. Um, she walked in and I had the room filled with lilies, her favorite flower…

lines.speaker: Aunt Lisa
lines.text: Oh that is so sweet!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shhh! I want to hear the rest!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then, Fred Astaire singing The Way You Look Tonight came on the sound system, and the lights came down. And I got down on one knee and written across the dome in the stars were the words "Will you marry me?"

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Rachel and Ross

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And the ring, was the size of my fist !

scenes.scene_description: Various oohs and ahhs

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah uh, Phoebe! Look umm, I want to apologize about before, okay? We were being jerks. Parker’s a nice guy and I’d like to get to know him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then you better do it now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because I’m going to kill him

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What-what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys were right. He’s just too excited about…everything. I mean I’m all for living life, but this is the Geller’s 35 th anniversary. Okay? Let’s call a spade a spade this party stinks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know I’m having the worst time. There was a 15-minute line for the buffet, and when I finally got up to the plates, I slipped on a giant booger!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you sure it wasn’t an oyster?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I guess it could’ve been, I didn’t really look at it. Y’know, I just wiped it on Chandler’s coat and got the hell out of there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He’s just such a great guy I’m so excited about him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey, you should be excited about him. There’s nothing wrong with him he’s a good guy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You think?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Ya know what I think; I think we were all just being too negative.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re right. You’re right, he’s just embracing life. We could all stand to be a little more like Parker. You know what? I am like him! I’m a sunny, positive person.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually, you have a little bit of an edge.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What’s that now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh look it’s Parker!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Look! It’s the bunny hop!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oooh I love it!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: You do?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you kidding? People acting like animals to music. Come on!

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Phoebe and Joey

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay it’s time for the toast! Umm now-now, I know that Ross usually gives the toast, but this year I’m going to do it.

scenes.scene_description: Cut to Monica, at the microphone

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no it’s going to be great. Really! Mom, Dad, when I got married, one of the things that made me sure I could do it was the amazing example the two of you set for me. For that and so many other things I want to say thank you. I know I probably don’t say it enough, but I love you. When I look around this room, I’m-I’m saddened by the thought of those who could not be here with us. Nana, my beloved grandmother who would so want to be here, but she can’t because she’s dead. As is our dog Chi-Chi. I mean look how cute she is. . Was. Do me a favor and pass this to my parents. Remember she’s dead. Okay, her and Nana, gone. Wow! Hey does anybody remember when Debra Winger had to say goodbye to her children in Terms of Endearment ? Didn’t see that? No movie fans?! You want to hear something sad? The other day I was watching 60 Minutes these orphans in Romania, who have been so neglected, they were incapable of love. You people are made of stone! Here’s to mom and dad! Whatever!
lines.stage_direction: Pretends to cry hoping her parents will join her. Holds up the picture and pretends to cry again To an old man by the stage. Chandler covers his ears Waits for people to cry, but doesn’t get any tears.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Thank you Monica that was uh, interesting. Wasn’t it interesting, Jack?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Why don’t I remember this dog?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Ross, why don’t you give us your toast now?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no, Mom, it’s just Monica this year.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: You’re not going to say anything? On our 35 th wedding anniversary

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, of course, Um… Um, everybody? Um, I-I just wanted to say…on behalf of my new bride, Rachel , and myself. Umm, that if…if in 35 years, we’re half as happy as you guys are, we’ll count ourselves the luckiest people in the world.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh Ross…

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I just wish Nana were alive to hear Ross’s toast.

scenes.scene_description: Everyone sighs

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: My God what a fantastically well lit hallway!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Can I get you something to drink? Like a water and Valium?

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I must say this apartment, its, its, There are no words…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh thank God.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: It’s a haven. A third-floor paradise. A modern-day Eden in the midst…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah? I know! I know! Uh huh? Listen why don’t we just um, sit and relax? You know just be with each other. Quietly!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: That sounds great. My God this is the most comfortable couch I’ve ever sat on in my entire life.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Let’s try something else, let’s play a game.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I love games!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shocking! Let’s play the game of who can stay quiet the longest.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Or… Jenga .

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But, let’s play this one first. And remember whoever talks first loses!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe’s apartment, Parker and her are entering.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I lose, now Jenga .

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Is something wrong?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wrong? Really? You know the word wrong. Everything isn’t perfect? Everything isn’t magical? Everything isn’t a glow with the light of a million fairies? They were just brake lights, Parker!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Well, excuse me for putting a good spin on a traffic jam!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don’t have to put a good spin on everything.

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: I’m sorry that’s who I am. I’m a positive person.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! I am a positive person. You are like Santa Clause on Prozac, at Disneyland , getting laid!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: So what do you want me to do, you want me to be more negative, less happy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Much less happy!

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Fine! Well then to quote Ross, "I’d better be going."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So long! Don’t let the best door in the world hit you in the ass on your way out!
lines.stage_direction: He exits and she slams the door behind him.

scenes.scene_description: They sit back

lines.speaker: Parker
lines.text: Isn’t this the most incredible fight you’ve ever had in your entire life?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh huh.

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on door, and Phoebe opens it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: …and then, we could’ve gone from the ceremony to the reception with you in the sidecar!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, it just wouldn’t have been feasible.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But having a dove place the ring on your finger would’ve been no problem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It was really fun being married to you tonight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! And! And, it was the easiest 400 bucks I’ve ever made.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Ross, can I uh, can I ask you something?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That proposal, at the planetarium…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, I know it was stupid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you kidding?! With the, with the lilies, and-and the song, and the stars! It was…really wonderful! Did you just make that up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, actually I thought about it when, when we were going out. It’s how I imagined I uh, I would ask you to marry me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that would’ve been very hard to say no too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s a good thing I didn’t do it, because it sounds like it would’ve been a very expensive wedding. Okay, good night

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Goodnight

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Rachel’s, they’re returning from the party.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Even if the sidecar had a windscreen so your hair wouldn’t get messed up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I will think about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s all I’m askin’

scenes.scene_description: They go off to their bedrooms

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay that’s it. I give up. At mom and dad’s 40 th anniversary, you’re the one giving the speech.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know I don’t understand why they didn’t cry. It was a beautiful speech.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, come on.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! All that stuff you said about true love, you were right, I mean, we did learn a lot from Mom and Dad! And that picture of Chi-Chi with her mischievous grin. And what you said about Nana. Ohh, yeah she really would’ve wanted to be there. And you know what? I think she was.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good God, Ross! How the hell do you do it?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Ross and Monica are there.

title: The One With Joey’s Interview

lines.speaker: Episodes Orginally Transcribed by
lines.text: Eric Aasen , guineapig , Josh Hodge , Aaron D. Howard-Miller , and Kiza Abuzahra .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, I’m in my apartment doing the Soap Opera Digest crossword puzzle, and guess who the clue is for three down.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Three down, Days Of Our Lives star blank Tribbiani. That’s me!! I’m blank!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How cool is this?! We know three down! I’m touching three down!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, everyone except Phoebe is there as Rachel enters carrying a magazine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah you are baby.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Three down knows I’m married, what’s three down doin’?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So did they call you to tell you your name’s gonna be in this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. They really like me over there. They want to do a big profile on me, but I said no.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why’d you say no?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Remember what happened the last time I did an interview for them? I said I write a lot of my own lines, and then the writers got mad and made my character fall down the elevator shaft. So who knows what I might say this time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If only there was something in your head to control the things you say.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on Joey! You will totally keep it in check this time, and plus y’know the publicity would be really good for your career! And you deserve that! And if you do the interview you can mention, oh I don’t know, gal pal Rachel Green?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is that gal pal spelled L-O-S-E-R?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, don’t listen to him. Please?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine! All right, I’ll do it. But hey! You guys have to be at the next table so you can stop me if I y’know, start to say something stupid.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just then or-or all the time, ‘cause we-we have jobs y’know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on! We will be there for you the whole time! Just remember gal pal Rachel Green. Ha-ha! I’m gonna be in Soap Opera Digest ! And not just in the dumb crossword puzzle. Seriously, proud of you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

scenes.scene_description: She has her hand on his shoulder.

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: I really appreciate you taking the time to do this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, not at all. Happy to do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You think we’re being obvious?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, we’re just four people with neck problems. You talk like this.
lines.stage_direction: Out of the sides of their mouths.

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: Y’know I think its great you wanted to meet here. Y’know when most people hear the magazine is paying for it they want to go to a big fancy restaurant.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually, I didn’t know the magazine was paying for it. Wouldn’t have mattered, I’m doing this for the fans, not for the free food.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Can I get you anything?

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: Umm, I’ll have a cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I’ll have all the muffins.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Shhh! We’re not talking.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Finally! Oh.

The crowd ahhs.

lines.text: I’m gonna get some coffee, anyone want anything?

lines.text: And a bagel with only…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I was just being polite!

lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mento?

lines.text: No thanks.

lines.text: So, as Joey’s friends, is there anything that you guys think our readers ought to know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, that’s gonna get you into Soap Opera Digest . Well I……I would just like to say that Joey truly has enriched the days of our lives.
lines.stage_direction: scoffs at him leans into the microphone again

lines.text: Umm, Phoebe Buffay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh okay, it’s P as in Phoebe, H as in hoebe, O as in oebe, E as in ebe, B as in bee-bee and E as in ‘Ello there mate!

lines.text: Oh, Joey! Sorry!

lines.text: Wow, you are a good friend, 'course the audition was this morning, and I didn't get it. But that was a hell of a kiss. Rachel is a very lucky girl.

lines.text: Okay. Now-now-now should I climb down your front so we’re face to face or-or should I climb down your back so we’re-we’re butt to face.

lines.text: I would say that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.text: So, let’s talk about women. I’m sure our female readers will be interested to know about your romantic life.

lines.text: Oh-oh-oh-oh, how I do it is, I look a woman up and down and say, "Hey, how you doin’?"

lines.text: Oh my God, introduce us!

lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: So what’s the final head count on my baby shower?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: About twenty, a couple people from work who had something else to do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Also both of your sisters called and neither can make it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! You mean they’re not coming to a social event where there’s no men and there’s no booze?! That’s shocking! I don’t care, as long as my mom’s here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, your mother!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! My mom’s not gonna be here?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, given that we forgot to invite her it would be an awfully big coincidence if she was.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well it wasn’t my fault, Phoebe was in charge of the invitations!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well I don’t, I don’t have a mother so often I forget that other people…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh give it a rest!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So my mother is not coming to my baby shower?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. Neither is mine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, y’know what? Don’t worry, okay? We’ll take care of it. We’ll call her. Just go home and get ready.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Please, make sure she comes. It’s really important to me, I mean it’s my mom!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. I know, what’s her number?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Go! I have it in my book. Go! Wait a minute! If you’re in charge of the invitations why am I the one who has to call her—Hello Mrs. Green! Hi, it’s Monica Geller.
lines.stage_direction: Rachel leaves and Monica calls Mrs. Green. To Phoebe

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh, hello Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, umm I know this is last minute, but we’ve decided to throw an impromptu baby shower for Rachel today.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: I know, my daughter’s told me about it when they received their impromptu invitations a month ago.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I’m sorry. I’m-I’m so sorry.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: For what dear? For not inviting me or lying about it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, my ass is sweating! Please! Please! Can you come? It’s today at four.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well all right. I’ll see you at four.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Isn’t it at three?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Son of a bitch!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Phoebe are preparing for Rachel’s baby shower.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe! You wanna shoot some hoops?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no, I can’t go. I’m practicing; I got an audition to be the host of a new game show.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh cool!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s great.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, and if I get it by day I’ll Dr. Drake Remoray, but by night I’ll be Joey Trrrribbiani!
lines.stage_direction: In a sexy voice In an announcer’s voice

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’ll be perfect for this! That’s already your name!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But the audition’s in a couple hours and I don’t even understand the game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well do you want some help?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh really? That’d be great! You guys can be the contestants!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Awesome!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I guess we can lose to junior high girls some other time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! Let’s play Bamboozled!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bamboozled?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, isn’t that a cool name?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. Uhh, okay. Our first contestant is Ross Geller. Why don’t you tell us a little something about you Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well uh, I-I’m a paleontologist. Umm, I-I live in New York. I have a son Ben. Uh, hi Ben! And uh…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I said a little bit Ross. Now, how about you Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well Joey, I’m a headhunter. I hook up out of work Soviet scientists with rogue third-world nations. Hi Rasputin!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Excellent! Let’s play Bamboozled! Chandler, you’ll go first. What is the capital of Columbia?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bogota.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s Ba-go-ta, but close enough. Now, you can either pass your turn to Ross or pick a Wicked Wango card.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What does a Wicked Wango card do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I should know that. Let’s see, just one moment please. Umm, here we are, a Wicked Wango card determines whether you go higher or lower.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Higher or lower than what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is embarrassing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can you believe how lame this is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m sorry, I don’t believe contestants are allowed to talk to each other.

scenes.scene_description: Joey's Apartment, Joey is reading a script as Ross and Chandler enter carrying a basketball.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I told the stripper to be here at five. That’s good right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You ordered a stripper for the shower?! That is totally inappropriate!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? He’s gonna be dressed as a baby! Oh hi Mrs. Green!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m so glad you could make it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, thank you so much. And again, we’re so sorry. We could not feel worse about it.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Try. There’s my little girl.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She’s still mad.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I know. Isn’t it great? One less person we have to make small talk with.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, Sandra’s mad at you too. It-it doesn’t bother you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No look, we’ve apologized twice! I can’t do anymore than that. I know you hate it when people are mad at you but you just have to be okay with it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I can do that. I gotta go powder my ass.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Look at that face! Just like when you were in high school! If I didn’t know better I’d say you were a cheerleader in trouble. Come on, let’s get some tea.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: Mrs. Green helps her up and they walk over and get some tea.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh my look at that. Only three weeks to go, now have you picked your nanny yet? Now I don’t want you to use your housekeeper ‘cause it would just split her focus.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh well actually gonna use a nanny and uh, I don’t even have a housekeeper.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: It’s like you’re a cave person. Rachel, you must get a nanny. You don’t know how overwhelming this is going to be. I mean when you were a baby I had full time help, I had Mrs. Kay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mrs. Kay! Oh yeah, she was sweet. She taught me Spanish. I actually think I remember some of it, tu madre es loca.
lines.stage_direction: I think that’s your mother’s crazy.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Such a sweet woman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, however great she was I just can’t afford that.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: I just had the greatest idea! I’m gonna come live with you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wh-wh-what? What?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh, I’m so happy I’m gonna do this for my little girl. Aw, look at you. You have tears in your eyes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes. Yes I do.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Rachel’s baby shower is underway. Monica and Phoebe are working in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right Ross you’re in the lead, would you like to take another question or spin the Wheel of Mayhem?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The wheel has not been my friend tonight Joey. Uh, I’ll take another question.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, this is gonna be tough. Hold your breath.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s okay, I’m ready.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No dude, you gotta hold your breath until you’re ready to answer the question.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is ridiculous, he’s not gonna hold his breath…
lines.stage_direction: Ross cuts him off by taking a deep breath and holding it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, what do you have a fear of if you suffer from this phobia, Tris…Holy cow, that’s a big word. Trisc… Seriously look at this thing. Chandler, how do you say that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let me see that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This one right here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Triscadecaphobia.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The fear of Triscuts !

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No, fear of the number 13.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fear of Triscuts ?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s possible, they have really sharp edges.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right Chandler, you’re up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, I-I believe I’m entitled to use my Angel Pass for a free turn?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This game makes no sense!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know what? You’re just upset because you’re losing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on Ross, I think we’re all losers here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. Chandler, you can either spin the wheel or pick a Google card.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let me think. Let me think—Oh! I don’t care.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You-you must choose Mr. Bing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Either, it makes no difference.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Choose, you jackass!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’ll take a card.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, you picked the Gimmie card! You get all of Ross’s points!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This game is kinda fun.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You don’t think it’s a little crazy that you get all my points just ‘cause you…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t think the contestants are supposed to speak to each other.

scenes.scene_description: Joey's Apartment, the guys are still playing Bamboozled.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why did you invite my mother?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She wants to move in with me and Ross to help take care of the baby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: For how long?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Eight weeks. I mean I love my mother, but my God, a long lunch with her is taxing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I personally would be honored if she wanted to live with me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She can’t hear you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? You guys, come on! What am I going to do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if you don’t want your mother to move in with you, just tell her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You’re right. You’re right. I mean I’m about to have a baby, I can tell my mother that I don’t want her to just be sleeping on my couch! Oh my God! She’s gonna want to sleep in my bed with me. This cannot happen!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s right. That is right, you go over there and tell her you don’t want her to live with you. Do not take no for an answer!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: She goes over to tell her mother.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is great! Now she’s gonna be mad at Rachel! Y’know what? And I’m just gonna swoop in there and be like the daughter she never had.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I have new respect for Chandler. All right everybody! It’s time to open the presents!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Yes! And I think that the first gift that Rachel opens should be from the grandmother of the baby, because you’re the most important person in this room. And in the world!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well uh, I don’t have a gift because I wasn’t invited until the last minute, but thank you so much for bringing that to everyone’s attention.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How about you less important people, let’s open your presents!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, the baby shower is continuing as Rachel walks over to Monica and Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mom that’s okay that you didn’t get you a gift!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well, I kinda did. Me. Eight weeks of me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. Okay, see mom, the truth is I can do this on my own.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Sweetheart I know you’re gonna be terrific mom, I just think you need a little help, especially at the beginning.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But mom, I really know what I’m doing. I can handle this.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Really? Remember Twinkles?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He was a hamster! I am not going to vacuum up my baby!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, come on Rach it’s present time! Y’know you’re the glue that’s holding this whole party together. It’s kinda falling apart here.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh look.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, this is from your friend at work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my gosh! Oh wow! Oh, I know what this is! Wait a minute. That can’t be right. Is that a beer bong for a baby?
lines.stage_direction: She’s holding an item with a large suction cup connected to a yellow plastic box, with a long narrow tube and bottle connected the yellow part.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Darling, that’s a breast pump!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did I say I was done guessing? Okay, thank you for that. Oh wow! What’s this?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: It’s a diaper genie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it dispenses clean diapers!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: No! It’s where you put the dirty ones!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that’s gross, why don’t you just take it outside and throw it in a dumpster?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh you’re gonna do that ten times a day?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! It goes ten times a day! What are we feeding this baby?! Indian food?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: No dear, that’s what babies do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, listen to your mother. She is very smart.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Plus, what are you planning on doing with the baby while you’re trotting out to the garbage ten times a day?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know, I’d leave it on the changing table? What?! What’d I do? What’d I do?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: You can’t leave a baby alone!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh come——Of course I know that. I mean of course you never leave a baby alone! I mean who would—she wouldn’t be safe as she would be with me, the baby dummy. Oh God, okay. Y’know what? I think opening the presents right now is a little overwhelming right now. So I think umm, I’m just gonna maybe open them a little bit later, but thank you all for coming. And for these beautiful gifts, and this basket is beautiful.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: It’s actually a bassinet.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay mommy, don’t ever leave me.

scenes.scene_description: Mrs. Green goes into the kitchen and Rachel follows her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: In what John Houston film would you hear this line, "Badges? We don’t need no stinkin’ badges!"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Treasure of the Sierra Madre !

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Correct! There’s a possible backwards bonus!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Madre Sierra the of Treasure!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’d like to go up the ladder of chance to the golden mud hut please.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wise choice, how many rungs?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Six!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That noise can only me one thing.
lines.stage_direction: makes a sound like a monkey

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hungry monkey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hungry monkey! Haaa! I’d like a Wicked Wango card!
lines.stage_direction: excited and simultaneously as Chandler To Chandler To Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, it’s an audio question, name this television theme song.
lines.stage_direction: Starts humming the theme to I Dream of Genie .

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Oh! Oh my God! Okay, I know this, give me-give me a second!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tell it to the Time Turtle!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Shut up! I Dream of Genie !

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Yes, you’re back in the lead!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’d like to spin the wheel!

scenes.scene_description: Joey's Apartment, the guys are still playing the game only everyone is really into it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! All right! Uh, umm, Super-Speedy Speed round!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Is there a hopping bonus?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course!

scenes.scene_description: Joey makes a sound like a game show wheel spinning with the pointer bouncing off of the bars on the wheel as it slows and comes to a stop.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who invented bifocals?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ben Franklin.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Correct! Which monarch has ruled Great Britain the longest?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Queen Victoria.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Correct again! But, you forgot to switch legs between questions, so no hopping bonus!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Noooo!!! Every time!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now, over to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’d like a Google Card.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! No! Google!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! Congratulations Ross, because Chandler, you’ve been Bamboozled!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nooo!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is the best game ever!!!

scenes.scene_description: Ross gets up and starts to hop on one leg.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So umm, you’re gonna stay with me as long as I need you?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Of course I am!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh mom, I swear I’m not an idiot. I’ve read all kinds of books on pregnancy and giving birth, but I-I just didn’t think to read the part about what to do when the baby comes. And-and then guess what? The baby’s coming and I don’t know what to do. Oh, can I throw up in my diaper genie?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: No. Sweetie, you’re gonna be fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait-wait where are you going? Where are you going?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: I’m going to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Now don’t worry! Everything’s gonna be okay.
lines.stage_direction: Hugs Rachel while she is standing and Rachel is sitting, seeing this Monica decides to join in on the hugging by hugging Mrs. Green from behind her back.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It is going to be okay! It was worth a shot.
lines.stage_direction: Mrs. Green glances over her shoulder and glares at Monica while she heads for the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Why are you all red and sweaty?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just Bamboozled Chandler! Which is not uh sexual thing. That was a quick shower.
lines.stage_direction: Flexes in victory while everyone stares at him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not if you were here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! It looks like we got a lot of good stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh we did, but my mom got us the greatest gift of all.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A Play-Dough Barber Shop?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. She’s going to live with us for eight weeks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! She’s gonna help us take care of the baby! Woo-hoo.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What—You’re not serious. I mean she’s a very nice woman, but there is no way we can take eight weeks of her. She’ll drive us totally crazy.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Hi Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi roomie!
lines.stage_direction: Hugs her and looks at Rachel.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, the baby shower has ended and everyone except for Mrs. Green have left who is talking to Rachel while Monica and Phoebe are cleaning up.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Hey Joey, hi! I’m Ray; I’m the producer of the show.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s a pleasure to meet you Ray.

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: And this is Duncan and Erin, they’re gonna help us out with the audition. So uh, let’s get the camera rolling.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rightie-O Ray!

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Whenever you’re ready.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello, I’m Joey Tribbiani! Let’s play Bamboozled! Erin, you get the first question! In hockey, who is known as The Great One?

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Wayne Gretzky.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Correct! Now, would you like to pick a Wicked Wango card or spin the Wheel of Mayhem?

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Uh Joey, didn’t your agents give you the revised rules? We’ve eliminated all of that. No wheel, no cards.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What—Why?!

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Uh well, the game was too complicated and research showed people didn’t follow it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well what’s complicated? You spin the Wheel of Mayhem to go up the Ladder of Chance. You go past the Mud Hut through the Rainbow Ring to get to the Golden Monkey; you yank his tail and boom! You’re in Paradise Pond!

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Yeah all that’s gone. It’s basically just a simple question and answer game now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well what’s fun about that? You expect me to be the host of a boring game that’s just people standing around answering questions?

lines.speaker: Ray
lines.text: Well, there’ll be women in bikinis holding up the scores.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Let’s play Bamboozled!

scenes.scene_description: Joey’s Audition, Joey is being shown in.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: …and all those dinosaur nick-knacks you have Ross, I thought they might be more at home in the garage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well we…we don’t have a garage.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Did I say garage? I meant garbage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know what? Maybe, Mrs. Green, it’s not absolutely vital that you live with us.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well Rachel needs help with the baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I do. I really do. I don’t know anything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m-I’m sure that’s not true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no? Pheebs? Monica? Do I know anything about babies?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, not a thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s frightening.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well uh, y’know what? Even if she doesn’t know anything, I do! I have a son. And his mother and I didn’t live together, and whenever he was with me I took care of him all the time, by myself.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: That’s true. You do have another child.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: With another woman. Have you no control Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s a different issue. Uh, the point is, when the baby comes I will be there to…to feed her and bathe her and change her. And more than that I want to do all those things.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Well then you really don’t need me to live with you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! Yes, you’re gonna be so missed.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: You’re gonna be a great father.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well you’re gonna be a wonderful grandma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello?! I still don’t know what the hell I’m doing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on, every first time mother feels that way. You’ll—you’re gonna pick it up. Hey! You will! Uh look, y’know when you first came to the city? You were this spoiled helpless little girl who-who still used daddy’s credit card. Do you remember?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I hope you’re going somewhere with this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look at you! What—You’re-you’re this big executive! You are much more capable than you give yourself credit for. I-I have no doubt you’re gonna be an incredible mother.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m telling you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: All right you two, I’m gonna get going.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh.
lines.stage_direction: Rachel and he start to stand up.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no sweetheart, you stay put. I’ll let myself out. It’s like I’m not here, which I almost wasn’t.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’re still so funny. You’re so funny. What do I do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nothing! You have apologized to her like a million times and she’s been nothing but terrible to you. And don’t forget you just threw her daughter a lovely, albeit slightly boring, shower, and she hasn’t even thanked you for it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know what? You’re-you’re right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I mean if you want to say anything to her, I’d tell her off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! I will! Mrs. Green? Mrs. Green! It is rude to leave a party without saying good-bye to the host! Yeah, and-and also when someone apologizes to you the decent thing to do is to accept it! Now what I did to you, it wasn’t on purpose! But what you’re during to me now is just plain spiteful!
lines.stage_direction: She ignores Monica and Monica follows her out into the hall with Phoebe in tow.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Green
lines.text: Spiteful?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s right! Maybe it’s time you took a good hard look at a mirror young lady…old lady…lady!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wrap it up, wrap it up, wrap it up…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So whenever you’re ready to apologize to me, I will forgive you. Good day! I can’t feel my legs!
lines.stage_direction: Monica and Phoebe reenter the apartment and Monica closes the door on a stunned Mrs. Green.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You were fantastic! I’m so proud of you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah? I’m proud of me too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You should be!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah could-could-could you get me something to drink?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You got it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Mrs. Green! Okay I’m really sorry!! I’m apologizing for the— Okay, I bit my tongue, but I’m still really sorry!
lines.stage_direction: When Phoebe turns around Monica runs out into the hall after Mrs. Green. She trips and falls down the stairs. Pause

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! I’m ready.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, I’ve done my studying and I really know my stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right then. Rachel Green! Let’s play Bamboozled! How do you test the temperature of the baby’s bath water?
lines.stage_direction: Gets up, in an announcer’s voice Reading from a note card.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, put your elbow in it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excellent! How do you put a baby down for a nap?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Full, dry, on its back, and no loose covers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s correct! This is an audio question, what do you do when the baby makes this sound?
lines.stage_direction: Makes a sound like someone is choking a cat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Check if it’s wet, check if it’s hungry, burp it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excellent! Excellent, now-now do you want another question or a Wicked Wango card?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A card! A card! I pick a card!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I’m sorry you’ve been Bamboozled! You’re gonna be a terrible mother! I’ve lost sight of why we’re doing this!
lines.stage_direction: Rachel stares at him agape. Rachel gets up and walks away.

title: The One With The Cooking Class

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey you guys I got some bad news.

lines.text: Well that’s no way to sell newspapers. Why don’t you try, "Extra! Extra! Read all about it!"

lines.text: Hey!

lines.text: Hey.

lines.text: Oh dear God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, this is horrible!

lines.text: I’m so sorry.

lines.text: I’m so humiliated!

lines.speaker: Monica

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: No!

lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! What?

lines.text: I am freaking out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are ya?

lines.text: My due date is in one week!

lines.text: What are you doing up?

lines.text: That is seven days!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no-no-no-no Ross! Please, come on we do not have any of the big stuff we need! We do not a changing table! We do not have a crib! We do not have a diaper service!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m serious.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay look, there’s nothing to worry about. We have plenty of time. There’s a great baby furniture store on west 10 th . Tomorrow, we will go there and we will get you everything that you need. Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Thank you. That’s great. Thank you. Wait-wait! Where on west 10 th ? Because there’s this really cute shoe store that has like this little…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay. If uh, if you’re gonna do this, then I’m gonna do that. So…
lines.stage_direction: interrupting her Points to the bathroom. Starts for the bathroom.

lines.text: Oh, wait Ross! I’m sorry, one more thing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, our situation. Y’know umm, what we mean to each other. And I mean we-we’re having this baby together, and we live together. Isn’t that, isn’t that weird?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well uh…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m just kidding! You can go pee!

lines.text: We said at the movies, but…

lines.text: Okay, I’ll see you there.

lines.text: Joey! Now that you’re here…

lines.text: Sure, I can hang out ‘til I have to meet ya. What uh—How come you’re not going?

lines.text: I thought you already have a job.

lines.text: And people say you don’t pay attention. No, this is a much better job. It’s vice-president of a company that does data reconfiguration and statistical factoring for other companies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s what I do now.

lines.text: What is it?

lines.text: You cooked him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. He teaches a course on food criticism at the New School, so before we go to the movies I wanna go by there and make him try my bouillabaisse again. Oh, I cannot wait to read the front page of the Post tomorrow! "Restaurant reviewer admits: I was wrong about Monica."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The front page? You really do live in your own little world, don’t ya?

lines.text: Oh.

lines.text: No-no-no! No, no, no, we’re not married.

lines.text: Oh. Oh yeah, don’t get to worked up over it. I mean it-it sounds like he’s a doctor, but he’s not.

lines.text: Oh no-no, I’m fascinated by paleontology. Have you read the new Walter Alvarez book?

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.text: Oh my God! Well the idea of a woman flirting with a-with a single man, we-we must alert the church elders!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! You really—You look great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, just don’t get your hopes up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe

lines.text: Oh you don’t know.

lines.text: What is it that I do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did you like me when we first met?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, I’m not gonna lie to ya, but I am gonna run away from you.

lines.text: Hi! Umm, I’m Monica Geller, I’m the chef at Alessandro’s.

lines.text: Still?

lines.text: I think the things that you said about me are really unfair, and I would like for you to give my bouillabaisse another chance.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Spoon? So, what do you think?

lines.text: I’m torn, between my integrity and my desire to avoid a beating. But I must be honest, your soap is abysmal.

lines.speaker: Joey

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Monica.

lines.speaker: Rachel

lines.text: Yeah, I’m just gonna grab my coat. And uh, and my whip. Y’know because of the Indiana Jones? Not-not because I’m-I’m into S&M. I’m not-I’m not into anything weird. Y’know? Just-just normal sex. So, I’m gonna grab my coat.

lines.text: …I think you’ll find if I come to work here, I don’t micro-manage. I don’t shy away from delegating.

lines.text: Um-hmm, that’s good to know. But let’s stop focusing on what you don’t do, and start focusing on what you do do.

lines.text: Uh-huh look, the only reason I can over here was to settle things between us! Okay? You’ve done a lot for me and my career, I wanted to pay you back so I took you to the premiere but you missed it! Okay, so how much do I owe you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Give me a number, I don’t want to owe you anything!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don’t owe me anything, I don’t want you money…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah-ah-ah! We’re doing this! Okay, now you got me my first set of head shots. Right, how much were those?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t know, five hundred dollars?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, five hundred dollars. What else?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well then there was the second set, the infamous booger head shots.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, so that’s another five hundred. Five hundred and five hundred, that’s…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you want a calculator?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Please!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here!
lines.stage_direction: Hands him one. Joey adds it up and discovers that he was right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, what else?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well uh, there was acting classes, stage combat classes, tap classes…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Which we’re still keeping under our hats!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh then there was that dialect coach who helped you with that play where you needed a southern accent. Which after twenty hours of lessons still came out Jamaican.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell are you talking about, "The south will rise again man."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, money well spent!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Okay, what else? Rent!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, two, three years of rent, utilities, food…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Okay, so I’m writing you a check for…So you fell asleep during my movie. Big deal right? How do you clear this thing?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is there as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, finish your enchilada.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross I—We tried all the spicy food. It’s not working.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay here, have one of these peppers. Oh ha… Oh God! So…so hot! Oh my— By the way, you don’t want to touch the pepper and then touch your eye.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am feeling nothing. Speaking of hot, watching you do that really makes me want to have sex with you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Stop it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh come on Ross, why are we wasting our time with this other stuff?! We know what’s gonna work! It’s doctor recommended!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m sorry, but we have to have some boundaries! My God, I’m dying.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh come on Ross, we’ve done it before we’ll do it again, it’ll be a nice way to bookend the pregnancy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is insane, I’m not gonna make love to you just so that you’ll go into labor.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Make love? What are you a girl?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Always a great way to get in a man’s pants.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But you will, you will be performing a service. Okay? Just-just think of me as a ketchup bottle, y’know you sometimes you have to bang on the end of it just to get something to come out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I love when you talk dirty to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know it. You’re right. That’s not sexy. Oh…Oh! Whoops! Oh, I seem to have dropped my fork. Let me just bed over and get it. Oh God!
lines.stage_direction: Drops a fork on the floor. Tries too, but can’t quite seem to make it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay enough! This is, this is not going to happen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on Ross! I’m miserable here! Come on! You started this, now you finish it! Come on wuss, make love to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Forget it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow! What now Ross you’re not gonna talk? How on earth will you ever annoy me? Oh wait a minute, I know. I mean you’d think the damn jalepeno would’ve cleared up your sinuses, but no!! That’s not enough… What are you doing?!
lines.stage_direction: Mimics his breathing. Ross jumps over and kisses her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m getting that baby out of you!

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Rachel's, Ross and Rachel are trying the home remedies again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no. No-no! I think my water just broke.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am good. Okay! Okay! Uh, I got the pillow! I got the bag! You got the keys?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! I got the keys! Okay! Okay!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We’re having a baby.

scenes.scene_description: They kiss again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I didn’t uh, really have time to read this part of the books, but do you think we have time to…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not so much.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: They hug and then kiss one more time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Two hundred.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s it. I’m done. I don’t care when the baby comes, no more betting.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is paying Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello? Uh-huh. Uh-huh. Okay. I guess we can bet one more time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is Rachel having the baby?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How did you know that?! Joey! Chandler!! It’s time!
lines.stage_direction: Runs to yell at Joey’s apartment.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They’re at the coffeehouse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know everything!! Oh wait, double or nothing. I bet you the baby is over seven pounds. I bet you it has hair. I bet you it’s a girl.
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe isn’t interested. She’s still not interested.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We know it’s a girl!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’ll give you really good odds.

scenes.scene_description: The phone rings and Monica answers it.

title: The One Where Rachel Has A Baby

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right! Yes!! From home to the hospital in under seven minutes! We did it!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, the hard part is truly over.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but come on, we’re off to a great start aren’t we? I knew I’d get you here fast, but this has got to be some kind of a record!

lines.text: Hi!

lines.text: Man, if only you’d gotten here sooner.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: I’m sorry. Semi-private rooms are all we have.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Just give us a second. Ross!
lines.stage_direction: They walk away from the desk.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Give her some money.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I really think they’re out of rooms.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They’re not!! Ross, they’re just saving them for the important people!! Okay?! What-what if I was the president?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well then we’d be in a lot of trouble, you don’t know where any countries are. Okay. Uh, say would you umm… Would you mind checking again to see if any umm, private rooms may have opened up?
lines.stage_direction: Rachel glares at him. He goes over to the desk followed by Rachel. Handing her some money

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: This is a hospital.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Y’know what? I’d have to say I really don’t care for your tone. And this is not the only hospital in this city and we have no problem to—Whoa! Oh gosh! Whoa!
lines.stage_direction: standing up She starts a contraction

lines.text: What-what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow! Ow! Contraction. Ow-ow! Ow-ow!

lines.text: Would you like to see a semi-private room?

lines.text: Thank you.

lines.text: Thank you. Well, I guess we have some time to kill.

lines.text: Oh.

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.text: Hey! Smile!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Okay, just breathe.

lines.text: Yeah?
