lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What-what-what’s that now?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. I’ve been thinking about it too, and I, I think we’re ready.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Are you kidding me?! You-you-you think we’re ready to have a baby now?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this is fun.

lines.text: You’re ready to have a baby? My boy’s all grown up!

lines.text: But you said you were ready too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah but I was just screwing with you to try to get your voice all high and weird like mine is now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but haven’t you wanted a kid like forever?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, just back off mister! Whoa. ‘Cause I am ready to have a baby. I just want Joey to be the father.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! Are you crazy?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s it! Right there! Is all I wanted!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that’s very—Really very-very okay.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Julie’s cervix is dilated a seven centimeters, that’s about four fingers. The doctor let me feel it myself.

lines.text: Oh. Oh wait no.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.text: No-no-don’t! Don’t leave me here with these people.

lines.text: Oh uh, I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel

lines.text: Hi! I’m so glad you’re here, but it’s gonna be a while. I-I wished you’d called first.

lines.text: Oh good.

lines.text: Okay, what’s up?

lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh hi dear!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thank you so much for coming. Ross, get in here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross.

Ross shrugs his shoulders to Rachel and Sick Bastard closes the screen all the way.

lines.text: Okay, hold on a sec.

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.text: No.

lines.text: All right.

lines.text: All right.

lines.text: Hi.

lines.text: Sure! I feel so bad for you; I broke my leg once too.

lines.text: Well what are we going to do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s okay honey, you’ll find a name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh, easy for you to say, you already know what your kids names are going to be.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I’ve had them picked out since I was fourteen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no, it’s gonna be named after some snack or baked good isn’t it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well tell us! What are they?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, okay. If it’s a boy it’s Daniel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And if it’s a girl?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t want to say.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, just tell us! We’re not gonna want it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. It’s Emma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Emma! See? I don’t want it.
lines.stage_direction: gasps Looks at the baby and starts to cry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Take it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s clearly an Emma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey, but you love that name.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but I love you more. Besides y’know, nothing goes with Bing. So I’m screwed. I mean… Oh, hi Emma. Yeah, that’s you. You’re our little Em. Oh what’s that honey? What? Oh, you want a little cousin? You want a cousin right now?!
lines.stage_direction: Rachel hands Emma to Monica. To Chandler

scenes.scene_description: Rachel starts crying again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was amazing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. Hey, do you realize we may have just changed our lives forever? We may have just started a family. Nine months from now we can be here, having our own baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And if not, we got to do it on a bucket.

scenes.scene_description: A Janitorial Closet, Monica and Chandler are emerging slowly.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Yoo-hoo! Aaron Litman-Neurolic would like to say hello to his future bride.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh! Wow! He kinda takes your breath away doesn’t he?
lines.stage_direction: Looks at Aaron and recoils in horror.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: He’s a keeper. How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I’m fine.
lines.stage_direction: Gasps in pain as she sits down.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Can I just say, I really admire what you’re doing. Just raising her all alone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I’m not doing it alone. I have Ross.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, sure. Now. But what happens when he meets somebody else and gets married?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then he gets a divorce, it’s Ross!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I’m telling you Rachel, listen to Janice. They all say they’re gonna be there until they start their real family.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well I—That’s never gonna happen with Ross.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh well that’s what I thought about my first husband, now I’m lucky if my kid gets to spend the weekend with her father and the twins and little Ms. New Boobs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I hate to be the one to say it, but honey you two are on your own.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well… That’s…y’know—That’s—We’ve been alone for the last twenty minutes we’re doing okay. Besides y’know what? I-I—Maybe we won’t be alone, ‘cause lately I-I—things have been happening between me and Ross, y’know? Right before I went into labor, we-we had this kiss. Y’know? So it might be the…the beginning of something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Janice!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who’s this little guy?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Say hello to Aaron, your future son-in-law.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no. No.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I’m gonna leave the three of you alone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Man! Did you see the kid on that nose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh. Y’know what I was, I was thinking about?
lines.stage_direction: Ross takes off his coat and sets in on a chair.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm…that kiss before we left the apartment. That was some-something huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, it really was. But we…we gotta be careful. We…we can’t let that happen again, y’know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean we don’t want to go down that road do we?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No, of course not. No. That’s why I brought it up. They didn’t have any sodas?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! I’m sorry, I was talking to this nurse, completely forgot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s all right. And so it begins.
lines.stage_direction: He goes to get her a soda.

scenes.scene_description: The Recovery Room, Rachel is putting Emma down for a nap.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is she in there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. She’s putting her down now, that’s her.
lines.stage_direction: Points to the nurse putting Emma now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look at Emma!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just can’t decide who she looks more alike, you or Rachel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh what are you kidding? She’s gorgeous, it’s all Rachel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m sorry, for the last time, why aren’t you two together again? No, I know. I know, because you’re not in that place. Which would be fine, except you totally are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s…it’s complicated okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah that’s true. Yeah, you love her. You always have. You have a child together. There is no right answer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, we’ve been together. Okay? And then apart, and then together, and then apart, and now we have a baby. It’s just if-if we got together again and it didn’t work out…I could never do that to Emma. I mean she-she thinking everything— Oh that’s…now me. What do they put something in the water in this place? Since Rachel and I we’re doing really, we’re doing really well right now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. I know. I know. I know, and if you try to make it more you might wreck it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, exactly.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right. Or you might get everything you’ve wanted since you were fifteen.

scenes.scene_description: Outside the Nursery, Ross is looking at Emma as Phoebe walks up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. I just saw a woman breast feeding both of her twins at the same time; it is like a freak show up here. What’s the matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is it? Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really it’s nothing. I’m just…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach come on, what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’ve just been thinking about how my baby and I are gonna be all alone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you talking about alone? What about Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please, he’ll be with his real family, the twins and little miss new boobs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, how long was I watching that woman?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m just saying that y’know, someday Ross is gonna meet somebody and…he’s gonna have his own life. Right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I guess so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just never thought I would raise this baby all by myself. Pretty dumb huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, listen to me, listen to me…you are never ever gonna be alone. Okay? I promise that’s not gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey. Honey what would I do without you?

scenes.scene_description: The Delivery Room, Rachel is in bed as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don’t have to worry about that okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hon can you grab me my other box of tissues? They’re right on that chair under Ross’s coat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: They hug.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My God.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey.

scenes.scene_description: He moves Ross’s coat to get the tissues and the engagement ring box Mrs. Geller gave him falls out of the pocket it was inside. Joey goes to one knee, picks up the box, opens it, and sees that it’s an engagement ring.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. Okay.

scenes.scene_description: He turns to face Rachel on one knee with the box open.
Joey is stunned.

title: The One Where Rachel Has A Baby

lines.speaker: Part I Written by
lines.text: Scott Silveri

lines.speaker: Part II Written by
lines.text: David Crane & Marta Kauffman

lines.speaker: Parts I & II Transcribed by
lines.text: Eric Aasen

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right! Yes!! From home to the hospital in under seven minutes! We did it!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, the hard part is truly over.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but come on, we’re off to a great start aren’t we? I knew I’d get you here fast, but this has got to be some kind of a record!

scenes.scene_description: The Hospital, Ross and Rachel, who’s in a wheelchair, are arriving in the waiting room for the maternity ward.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh you made it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute! How-how the hell did you beat us here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We took a cab. Did you guys walk?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: N… No! We took a cab too, but I did test runs!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe and Monica walk in from getting some coffee.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! You made it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay is there…some kind of magic tunnel to this hospital?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you stay here and talk, I’m gonna go have a baby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Okay. Umm hi, this is Rachel Green. I’m Ross Geller. We-we called from the car.
lines.stage_direction: To the nurse behind the desk.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Right! We have a semi-private labor room waiting for you. So in just a minute…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa! I’m sorry, semi-private? We , we asked for a private room.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Yes, I see that here. Unfortunately we can’t guarantee a private room and currently they’re all unavailable.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Man, if only you’d gotten here sooner.
lines.stage_direction: Ross turns and glares at him.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: I’m sorry. Semi-private rooms are all we have.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Just give us a second. Ross!
lines.stage_direction: They walk away from the desk.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Give her some money.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I really think they’re out of rooms.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They’re not!! Ross, they’re just saving them for the important people!! Okay?! What-what if I was the president?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well then we’d be in a lot of trouble, you don’t know where any countries are. Okay. Uh, say would you umm… Would you mind checking again to see if any umm, private rooms may have opened up?
lines.stage_direction: Rachel glares at him. He goes over to the desk followed by Rachel. Handing her some money

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: This is a hospital.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Y’know what? I’d have to say I really don’t care for your tone. And this is not the only hospital in this city and we have no problem to—Whoa! Oh gosh! Whoa!
lines.stage_direction: standing up She starts a contraction

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What-what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow! Ow! Contraction. Ow-ow! Ow-ow!
lines.stage_direction: Sits back down. Starts breathing heavily.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Would you like to see a semi-private room?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it couldn’t hurt to look.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey enter from the vending machines carrying sodas.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: Well you’re only two centimeters dilated and we need to get to ten. It’ll be a while.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Dr. Long
lines.text: I’ll be back in an hour to check you again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. Well, I guess we have some time to kill.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, guess so. Whew! Check these out! Never done this before.
lines.stage_direction: He’s looking at the stirrups on the other bed in the room and Rachel groans. Ross then hops into the bed and puts his legs into the stirrups.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah well it looks great!

scenes.scene_description: The Semi-Private Labor Room, Rachel is in bed, Ross is fooling around, and Dr. Long is checking on Rachel.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Thanks.

scenes.scene_description: A nurse shows another couple into the room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! Hi, I’m uh Ross. I’m here to ruin this magical day for you.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh no-no, not at all.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Don’t worry about it.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Marc Coreger, this is my wife Julie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Julie.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Oh hi Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Hi. Is this your first?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah it is.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Well, little Jamie here is our third. So, if you have questions or you need anything at all, just holler.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s so sweet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm say, I-I opened this earlier but let me give you guys some privacy.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: No nonsense! We’re all in this together.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Yeah, we are going to share every moment of this with you. And I think we’re gonna have some fun.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Hey! Smile!
lines.stage_direction: Points his camera at Ross and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, I really don’t want any——Oh! Thank you. Oh. Oh Ross…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Here comes another contraction.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Okay, just breathe.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Oh honey, I think I’m having one too!

scenes.scene_description: They stop when they see Ross who has to struggle to get out of the bed.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Look at this! There we go!

scenes.scene_description: During the mutual contraction Julie takes a moment to point out they’re having a contraction at the same time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh wow, three hours and still no baby. Ugh, the miracle of birth sure is a snooze fest.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, you wanna see something?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure! What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, this is going to be fun. Watch me freak out Chandler. Honey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen uh, I-I’ve been doing some thinking, and I don’t know whether it’s because we’re here or Rachel’s giving birth but umm, I think we should try to have a baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What-what-what’s that now?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. I’ve been thinking about it too, and I, I think we’re ready.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Are you kidding me?! You-you-you think we’re ready to have a baby now?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this is fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You’re ready to have a baby? My boy’s all grown up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But you said you were ready too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah but I was just screwing with you to try to get your voice all high and weird like mine is now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but haven’t you wanted a kid like forever?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, just back off mister! Whoa. ‘Cause I am ready to have a baby. I just want Joey to be the father.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! Are you crazy?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s it! Right there! Is all I wanted!

scenes.scene_description: The Waiting Room, the rest of the gang is lounging around.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: I am so sorry. The doctor insisted on closing the curtain for the exam.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that’s very—Really very-very okay.

lines.speaker: Marc
lines.text: Julie’s cervix is dilated a seven centimeters, that’s about four fingers. The doctor let me feel it myself.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Have you felt Rachel’s cervix Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I don’t think we’ll be doing that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We’re not gonna do that.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Well, if you like you can feel Rachel’s and then feel mine to compare.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Am I interrupting?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh yes! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh wait no.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Later.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no-don’t! Don’t leave me here with these people.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh uh, I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No Ross! Ross! Ross! My child has no father!

scenes.scene_description: The Semi-Private Labor Room, time lapse, Ross is massaging out a cramp on Rachel’s hip as Marc opens up the privacy screen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! I’m so glad you’re here, but it’s gonna be a while. I-I wished you’d called first.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh that’s all right, I’m coming back later with your father.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh good.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I actually needed to talk to you before the birth.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, what’s up?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I brought something that I want to give you, assuming of course that you want it.
lines.stage_direction: She holds up an engagement ring.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ma, you’re asking me to marry you?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: This is your grandmother’s engagement ring, I want you to give it to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mom no, come on! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Just hear me out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: N-no! Okay? We’ve been through this! We’re not gonna get married just because she’s pregnant, okay?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Honestly! Ross, this isn’t just some girl you picked up in a bar and humped. A child should have a family.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mom, y’know what? I-I can’t deal with this right now. I’m sorry…

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Just…think about it. If you don’t, I’ll talk more about humping.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gimmie!
lines.stage_direction: Takes the ring and puts it in his coat pocket as Rachel enters the hallway.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh hi dear!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thank you so much for coming. Ross, get in here!

scenes.scene_description: The Hallway, Ross comes out and hugs Mrs. Geller.
Mrs. Geller leaves as Ross re-enters the room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: …she came and dragged me out of the labor room to ask me why I’m not with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Why aren’t you with Rachel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you kidding? Look, we’re not gonna be together just because we’re having a baby. Okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But y’know what? It just seems that you two belong together.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, stop it! I can’t deal with this right now. I have to go have a baby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right. And with who again?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: God. He’s crazy! Why doesn’t he want to be with Rachel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean seriously, she’s like the perfect woman. I mean I know she turned me down, but if she hadn’t and wanted to be with me, I would take her in my arms and… I haven’t bummed you guys out like this in a while have I?

scenes.scene_description: The Waiting Room, Ross is explaining to the gang what happened with his mother.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey. Who’s that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: New people.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What happened to the Disgustingtons?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: They’re having their baby! It’s not fair Ross we got here first! Right after you left they wheeled her off into delivery. Oh but not before she gave me a juicy shot of little Jamie just crowning away.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Sorry. So uh, how are the new people?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well they have uh, some unusual pet names for each other. Including umm, evil bitch and uh, sick bastard. Oh God oh! Contraction!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh! Ow!!

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: Are you looking at her?!

lines.speaker: Sick Bastard
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: Don’t you look at her you sick bastard!

lines.speaker: Sick Bastard
lines.text: Honey I swear! I wasn’t looking at her!

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: She’s in labor! You like that you sick son of a bitch!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm. Umm, I’m-I’m just gonna—

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: See? See? It was because you were looking fat pervert!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no, I’m…I’m sure no one was looking. Just want some privacy.
lines.stage_direction: He closes the screen and stares wide-eyed at Rachel.

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: You miss your girlfriend?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just ignore them.

scenes.scene_description: The Semi-Private Labor Room, Ross is returning to find another couple has taken the place of Marc and Julie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He’s looking at me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! You wanna live to see your baby?!

lines.speaker: Evil Bitch
lines.text: Don’t you talk to my husband like that you stupid bastard!

scenes.scene_description: Sick Bastard sits down in a chair that enables him to look around the screen and stare at Rachel.
Ross shrugs his shoulders to Rachel and Sick Bastard closes the screen all the way.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good God! If you want a baby so bad just go steal it!

scenes.scene_description: Outside the Nursery, Chandler is looking at the babies as Monica walks up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is going on with you? Since when are you so crazy about babies?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m not crazy about babies. I’m crazy about us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, we’ve always talked about having babies someday. I’m not saying it has to be right now, but I’m starting to think that we can handle it. We’re good. We’re really good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We are pretty good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But nothing has to happen until your ready.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well maybe I’m ready now. I mean, it’s a little scary, but maybe it’s right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! It’s not right! We’re not ready to have a kid now!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m kidding. This is going to be fun.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So we’re gonna try? I mean, are we trying?

lines.speaker: Chandler

lines.text: It’s incredible, I mean one minute she’s inside you and then 47 hours later here she is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She looks so real! Y’know what I mean! She’s this whole tiny little person. She already has eyelashes and knees and…uh-oh.
lines.stage_direction: taking the baby The gang looks at him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no-no, no for I second there I counted six fingers, but one was from the other hand so we’re good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, my turn. My turn. Oh! You’re so cute! Oh, I could squeeze your little head! I won’t.
lines.stage_direction: Joey hands her to Phoebe. Pause

scenes.scene_description: The Recovery Room, Ross is taking pictures of Rachel holding the baby as the rest of the gang enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What’s the matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh nothing I… Sorry, I just can’t stop crying.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The doctor says it’s completely normal with all the hormones. Plus, you-you’re sleep deprived.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So? You guys are all sleep deprived. I don’t see you weeping because you put your slippers on the wrong feet. Oh God.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What’s the matter now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I was reliving it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, do you know what you’re gonna call her yet?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, wait a minute it’s not gonna be Baby Girl? I thought that was so original!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh actually, we-we’ve narrowed it down to two names.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and y’know what? I love them both, so why don’t you just pick one and that’ll be it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Umm, okay uh…everyone…this…is Isabella. What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s not her name! I’m sorry, she just doesn’t feel like an Isabella.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So then I guess Ferdinad is out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What was the other one Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, Delilah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh great! Suddenly she sounds like a biblical whore.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I guess we’re back to uh, Baby Girl.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well what are we going to do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s okay honey, you’ll find a name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh, easy for you to say, you already know what your kids names are going to be.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I’ve had them picked out since I was fourteen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no, it’s gonna be named after some snack or baked good isn’t it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well tell us! What are they?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, okay. If it’s a boy it’s Daniel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And if it’s a girl?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t want to say.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, just tell us! We’re not gonna want it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. It’s Emma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Emma! See? I don’t want it.
lines.stage_direction: gasps Looks at the baby and starts to cry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Take it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s clearly an Emma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey, but you love that name.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, but I love you more. Besides y’know, nothing goes with Bing. So I’m screwed. I mean… Oh, hi Emma. Yeah, that’s you. You’re our little Em. Oh what’s that honey? What? Oh, you want a little cousin? You want a cousin right now?!
lines.stage_direction: Rachel hands Emma to Monica. To Chandler

scenes.scene_description: Rachel starts crying again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was amazing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. Hey, do you realize we may have just changed our lives forever? We may have just started a family. Nine months from now we can be here, having our own baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And if not, we got to do it on a bucket.

scenes.scene_description: A Janitorial Closet, Monica and Chandler are emerging slowly.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Yoo-hoo! Aaron Litman-Neurolic would like to say hello to his future bride.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh! Wow! He kinda takes your breath away doesn’t he?
lines.stage_direction: Looks at Aaron and recoils in horror.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: He’s a keeper. How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I’m fine.
lines.stage_direction: Gasps in pain as she sits down.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Can I just say, I really admire what you’re doing. Just raising her all alone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I’m not doing it alone. I have Ross.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, sure. Now. But what happens when he meets somebody else and gets married?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then he gets a divorce, it’s Ross!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I’m telling you Rachel, listen to Janice. They all say they’re gonna be there until they start their real family.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well I—That’s never gonna happen with Ross.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh well that’s what I thought about my first husband, now I’m lucky if my kid gets to spend the weekend with her father and the twins and little Ms. New Boobs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I hate to be the one to say it, but honey you two are on your own.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well… That’s…y’know—That’s—We’ve been alone for the last twenty minutes we’re doing okay. Besides y’know what? I-I—Maybe we won’t be alone, ‘cause lately I-I—things have been happening between me and Ross, y’know? Right before I went into labor, we-we had this kiss. Y’know? So it might be the…the beginning of something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Janice!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who’s this little guy?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Say hello to Aaron, your future son-in-law.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no. No.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I’m gonna leave the three of you alone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Man! Did you see the kid on that nose?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh. Y’know what I was, I was thinking about?
lines.stage_direction: Ross takes off his coat and sets in on a chair.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm…that kiss before we left the apartment. That was some-something huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, it really was. But we…we gotta be careful. We…we can’t let that happen again, y’know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean we don’t want to go down that road do we?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No, of course not. No. That’s why I brought it up. They didn’t have any sodas?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! I’m sorry, I was talking to this nurse, completely forgot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s all right. And so it begins.
lines.stage_direction: He goes to get her a soda.

scenes.scene_description: The Recovery Room, Rachel is putting Emma down for a nap.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is she in there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. She’s putting her down now, that’s her.
lines.stage_direction: Points to the nurse putting Emma now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look at Emma!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just can’t decide who she looks more alike, you or Rachel?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh what are you kidding? She’s gorgeous, it’s all Rachel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m sorry, for the last time, why aren’t you two together again? No, I know. I know, because you’re not in that place. Which would be fine, except you totally are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s…it’s complicated okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah that’s true. Yeah, you love her. You always have. You have a child together. There is no right answer.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, we’ve been together. Okay? And then apart, and then together, and then apart, and now we have a baby. It’s just if-if we got together again and it didn’t work out…I could never do that to Emma. I mean she-she thinking everything— Oh that’s…now me. What do they put something in the water in this place? Since Rachel and I we’re doing really, we’re doing really well right now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. I know. I know. I know, and if you try to make it more you might wreck it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, exactly.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right. Or you might get everything you’ve wanted since you were fifteen.

scenes.scene_description: Outside the Nursery, Ross is looking at Emma as Phoebe walks up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. I just saw a woman breast feeding both of her twins at the same time; it is like a freak show up here. What’s the matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is it? Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really it’s nothing. I’m just…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach come on, what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’ve just been thinking about how my baby and I are gonna be all alone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you talking about alone? What about Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please, he’ll be with his real family, the twins and little miss new boobs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, how long was I watching that woman?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m just saying that y’know, someday Ross is gonna meet somebody and…he’s gonna have his own life. Right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I guess so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just never thought I would raise this baby all by myself. Pretty dumb huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, listen to me, listen to me…you are never ever gonna be alone. Okay? I promise that’s not gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey. Honey what would I do without you?

scenes.scene_description: The Delivery Room, Rachel is in bed as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don’t have to worry about that okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hon can you grab me my other box of tissues? They’re right on that chair under Ross’s coat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: They hug.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My God.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey.

scenes.scene_description: He moves Ross’s coat to get the tissues and the engagement ring box Mrs. Geller gave him falls out of the pocket it was inside. Joey goes to one knee, picks up the box, opens it, and sees that it’s an engagement ring.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. Okay.

scenes.scene_description: He turns to face Rachel on one knee with the box open.
Joey is stunned.
